revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Yoshida Shrine |image = [[File:Yoshida shrine.jpg|270px|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) worship hall]]
Worship Hall |address = [[2 Sekiya-cho, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture|愛知県豊橋市関屋町2番地]] |coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-23|display=inline,title}} |deities = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] |rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] |established = (Tradition) [[702 AD]] (Taihō 2) |main_hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] |alias = |annual_festival = [[July]] (third [[Friday]], [[Saturday]], and [[Sunday]]) |rituals = Yoritomo Procession |map = {{Location map|Japan Aichi Prefecture|label=Yoshida Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Yoshida Shrine''' (吉田神社) is a Shinto shrine located at {{ill|Sekiya-cho|ja|関屋町}}, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture. Under the [[Modern Shrine Ranking System|modern shrine ranking system]], it was designated a Prefectural Shrine in {{ill|1921|ja|大正11年}}, having been elevated from a {{ill|village shrine|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == The primary deity enshrined is: * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (素戔嗚尊){{Cite book|title=Rediscovering Our Hometown Guidebook: Learning More About Toyohashi, Vol. 4|date=March 19, 2009|publisher=Toyohashi City Public Relations Division|page=3}} Additionally, [[Empress Jitō]] is enshrined as a complementary deity. == History == [[File:Yoshida, Tenno Festival on the Fifteenth Day of the Sixth Month LACMA M.66.35.23.jpg|thumb|Yoshida Shrine Tennō Festival]] The shrine is said to originate from the temporary palace established during Empress Jitō's visit to Mikawa Province in 702 (Taihō 2). Following her passing, the palace was converted into a shrine dedicated to her. Early records, such as the ''Wakan Sansai Zue,'' refer to it as a "Tennō Shrine enshrining Empress Jitō." During the Edo period, shrine legends record ceremonies honoring her. In 1124 (Tenji 1), during an epidemic, the {{ill|Yokochi clan|ja|横地氏}}, shrine priests, invited [[Gozu Tennō]], the guardian deity of {{ill|Jetavana Monastery|ja|祇園精舎}}, from the {{ill|Kanshin-in|ja|感神院}} (present-day {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}}) to suppress the disease. Subsequently, the shrine was called "Gozu Tennō Shrine" or "Yoshida Tennō Shrine." In 1178 (Jishō 2), during his exile in Izu, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} sent an envoy, Suzuki Jūrō Mototoshi, to pray at Yoshida Shrine. In 1186 (Bunji 2), he dispatched Ishida Jirō Tamehisa for the same purpose. During the Kenkyū era, he ordered Adachi Tōkurō Morinaga, the protector of Mikawa, to rebuild the shrine. In 1507 (Eishō 3), following the construction of {{ill|Yoshida Castle|ja|吉田城 (三河国)}}, {{ill|Makino Kokuhaku|ja|牧野古白}} rebuilt the shrine as the castle’s guardian shrine. In 1548 (Tenbun 16), {{ill|Imagawa Yoshimoto|ja|今川義元}} donated a mikoshi, initiating the shrine’s mikoshi procession. The inscription on the mikoshi’s ridgepole plaque, written by {{ill|Taigen Sessai|ja|太原雪斎}}, reads: “Peace throughout the land, safety of the nation, harmony among the people.” During the Edo period, the shrine received patronage from successive {{ill|lords of Yoshida Domain|ja|三河吉田藩}}, resulting in several reconstructions. In 1837 (Tenpō 6), it received the highest rank of ''Shoichii'' (Senior First Rank). The shrine’s festivals, influenced by {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭}} in Kyoto, were well-known and recorded in various festival rankings. Following the abolition of domains during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the shrine’s name changed to Yoshida Shrine under the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, and it was reorganized with Susanoo-no-Mikoto as its deity. == Toyohashi Gion Festival == {{main|Toyohashi Gion Festival}} == Precincts == File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Torii.JPG|Entrance Stone Torii File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Altar.JPG|Inner Sanctuary File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Auxiliary Shrines.JPG|Kin Kanmaru and Castle Guardian Inari Shrine File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Fireworks Monument.JPG|Handheld Fireworks Origin Monument File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Handheld Fireworks.JPG|Large Fireworks File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Precinct View.JPG|View from the Precinct File:Toyohashi Gion Matsuri.JPG|Handheld Fireworks Dedication at Toyohashi Gion Festival == Auxiliary Shrines == * Inari Shrine ** Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]] * Izawa Shrine ** Enshrines [[Isasazutome-no-Mikoto]] * Miketsu Shrine ** Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]] and [[Tamatsuhime-no-Mikoto]] == Cultural Properties == [[File:Yoshida Tennō Shrine Festival Painting.jpg|580px|thumb|Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting]] '''Toyohashi City Designated Folk Cultural Properties''' * Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting (including donor inscriptions){{Cite web|date=May 7, 2021|url=http://www.toyohashi-bihaku.jp/?p=14012|title=Yoshida Shrine Items Designated as City Cultural Properties|publisher=Toyohashi City Museum of Art and History|accessdate=April 19, 2022}} : Dimensions: approximately 1.5 m by 3.6 m{{Cite web|date=May 8, 2021|url=http://www.higashiaichi.co.jp/news/detail/7922|title="Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting" Designated as a Folk Cultural Property|publisher=Higashi Aichi Newspaper|accessdate=April 19, 2022}}. Painted by Hatake Achi in 1890 (Meiji 23). The painting depicts festival scenes from the late Edo to early Meiji periods. '''Toyohashi City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties''' * Lion Heads: Created during the Nanboku-chō to Muromachi periods. * Demon Faces: From the Muromachi period. * Mikoshi Ridgepole Plaques: Five plaques dating from 1547 (Tenbun 16) to 1852 (Kaei 5). * Pair of Lions: From the Kamakura period. == Information == ; Address * {{ill|2 Sekiya-cho, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県豊橋市関屋町2番地}} ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|Toyohashi Railroad|ja|豊橋鉄道}}, {{ill|Shiyakusho-mae Station (Aichi)|ja|市役所前停留場}} (approx. 5-minute walk) * Nearest station: {{ill|Meitetsu|ja|名古屋鉄道}}, {{ill|Toyohashi Station|ja|豊橋駅}} (approx. 20-minute walk) == Notes == {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Toyohashi Gion Festival|ja|豊橋祇園祭}} * {{ill|Handheld Fireworks|ja|手筒花火}} * {{ill|Sugimori Hachiman Shrine|ja|杉森八幡社}} * {{ill|Watanabe Koka|ja|渡辺小崋}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoshida-jinja (Toyohashi)}} * [http://toyohashi-yoshida.com Yoshida Shrine Official Website] * [http://toyohashigion.org/index.html Toyohashi Gion Festival Support Association] * [http://www.honokuni.or.jp/toyohashi/spot/000062.html Toyohashi Tourism Convention Association] * [https://www.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/wo06/wo06_3145_56/] Festival Guidebook * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DwAR4KaisJI Yoshida Shrine Toyohashi Gion Festival Handheld Fireworks] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties of Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Gion Shrines]] [[Category:Yasaka Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyohashi City]] [[Category:History of Toyohashi City]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORTYoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:吉田神社 (豊橋市)]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:吉田神社]]

Following the abolition of domains during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the shrine’s name changed to Yoshida Shrine under the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, and it was reorganized with Susanoo-no-Mikoto as its deity. == Toyohashi Gion Festival == {{main|Toyohashi Gion Festival}} == Precincts == File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Torii.JPG|Entrance Stone Torii File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Altar.JPG|Inner Sanctuary File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Auxiliary Shrines.JPG|Kin Kanmaru and Castle Guardian Inari Shrine File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Fireworks Monument.JPG|Handheld Fireworks Origin Monument File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Handheld Fireworks.JPG|Large Fireworks File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Precinct View.JPG|View from the Precinct File:Toyohashi Gion Matsuri.JPG|Handheld Fireworks Dedication at Toyohashi Gion Festival == Auxiliary Shrines == * Inari Shrine ** Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]] * Izawa Shrine ** Enshrines [[Isasazutome-no-Mikoto]] * Miketsu Shrine ** Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]] and [[Tamatsuhime-no-Mikoto]] == Cultural Properties == [[File:Yoshida Tennō Shrine Festival Painting.jpg|580px|thumb|Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting]] '''Toyohashi City Designated Folk Cultural Properties''' * Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting (including donor inscriptions){{Cite web|date=May 7, 2021|url=http://www.toyohashi-bihaku.jp/?p=14012|title=Yoshida Shrine Items Designated as City Cultural Properties|publisher=Toyohashi City Museum of Art and History|accessdate=April 19, 2022}} : Dimensions: approximately 1.5 m by 3.6 m{{Cite web|date=May 8, 2021|url=http://www.higashiaichi.co.jp/news/detail/7922|title="Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting" Designated as a Folk Cultural Property|publisher=Higashi Aichi Newspaper|accessdate=April 19, 2022}}. Painted by Hatake Achi in 1890 (Meiji 23). The painting depicts festival scenes from the late Edo to early Meiji periods. '''Toyohashi City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties''' * Lion Heads: Created during the Nanboku-chō to Muromachi periods. * Demon Faces: From the Muromachi period. * Mikoshi Ridgepole Plaques: Five plaques dating from 1547 (Tenbun 16) to 1852 (Kaei 5). * Pair of Lions: From the Kamakura period. == Information == ; Address * {{ill|2 Sekiya-cho, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県豊橋市関屋町2番地}} ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|Toyohashi Railroad|ja|豊橋鉄道}}, {{ill|Shiyakusho-mae Station (Aichi)|ja|市役所前停留場}} (approx. 5-minute walk) * Nearest station: {{ill|Meitetsu|ja|名古屋鉄道}}, {{ill|Toyohashi Station|ja|豊橋駅}} (approx. 20-minute walk) == Notes == {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Toyohashi Gion Festival|ja|豊橋祇園祭}} * {{ill|Handheld Fireworks|ja|手筒花火}} * {{ill|Sugimori Hachiman Shrine|ja|杉森八幡社}} * {{ill|Watanabe Koka|ja|渡辺小崋}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoshida-jinja (Toyohashi)}} * [http://toyohashi-yoshida.com Yoshida Shrine Official Website] * [http://toyohashigion.org/index.html Toyohashi Gion Festival Support Association] * [http://www.honokuni.or.jp/toyohashi/spot/000062.html Toyohashi Tourism Convention Association] * [https://www.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/wo06/wo06_3145_56/] Festival Guidebook * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DwAR4KaisJI Yoshida Shrine Toyohashi Gion Festival Handheld Fireworks] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties of Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Gion Shrines]] [[Category:Yasaka Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyohashi City]] [[Category:History of Toyohashi City]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:吉田神社 (豊橋市)]] [[Category:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)]][[Category:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:吉田神社]]

{{Shrine |name = Yoshida Shrine |image = [[File:Yoshida shrine.jpg|270px|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) worship hall]]
Worship Hall |address = [[2 Sekiya-cho, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture|愛知県豊橋市関屋町2番地]] |coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-23|display=inline,title}} |deities = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] |rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] |established = (Tradition) [[702 AD]] (Taihō 2) |main_hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] |alias = |annual_festival = [[July]] (third [[Friday]], [[Saturday]], and [[Sunday]]) |rituals = Yoritomo Procession |map = {{Location map|Japan Aichi Prefecture|label=Yoshida Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Yoshida Shrine''' (吉田神社) is a Shinto shrine located at {{ill|Sekiya-cho|ja|関屋町}}, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture. Under the [[Modern Shrine Ranking System|modern shrine ranking system]], it was designated a Prefectural Shrine in {{ill|1921|ja|大正11年}}, having been elevated from a {{ill|village shrine|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == The primary deity enshrined is: * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (素戔嗚尊){{Cite book|title=Rediscovering Our Hometown Guidebook: Learning More About Toyohashi, Vol. 4|date=March 19, 2009|publisher=Toyohashi City Public Relations Division|page=3}} Additionally, [[Empress Jitō]] is enshrined as a complementary deity. == History == [[File:Yoshida, Tenno Festival on the Fifteenth Day of the Sixth Month LACMA M.66.35.23.jpg|thumb|Yoshida Shrine Tennō Festival]] The shrine is said to originate from the temporary palace established during Empress Jitō's visit to Mikawa Province in 702 (Taihō 2). Following her passing, the palace was converted into a shrine dedicated to her. Early records, such as the ''Wakan Sansai Zue,'' refer to it as a "Tennō Shrine enshrining Empress Jitō." During the Edo period, shrine legends record ceremonies honoring her. In 1124 (Tenji 1), during an epidemic, the {{ill|Yokochi clan|ja|横地氏}}, shrine priests, invited [[Gozu Tennō]], the guardian deity of {{ill|Jetavana Monastery|ja|祇園精舎}}, from the {{ill|Kanshin-in|ja|感神院}} (present-day {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}}) to suppress the disease. Subsequently, the shrine was called "Gozu Tennō Shrine" or "Yoshida Tennō Shrine." In 1178 (Jishō 2), during his exile in Izu, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} sent an envoy, Suzuki Jūrō Mototoshi, to pray at Yoshida Shrine. In 1186 (Bunji 2), he dispatched Ishida Jirō Tamehisa for the same purpose. During the Kenkyū era, he ordered Adachi Tōkurō Morinaga, the protector of Mikawa, to rebuild the shrine. In 1507 (Eishō 3), following the construction of {{ill|Yoshida Castle|ja|吉田城 (三河国)}}, {{ill|Makino Kokuhaku|ja|牧野古白}} rebuilt the shrine as the castle’s guardian shrine. In 1548 (Tenbun 16), {{ill|Imagawa Yoshimoto|ja|今川義元}} donated a mikoshi, initiating the shrine’s mikoshi procession. The inscription on the mikoshi’s ridgepole plaque, written by {{ill|Taigen Sessai|ja|太原雪斎}}, reads: “Peace throughout the land, safety of the nation, harmony among the people.” During the Edo period, the shrine received patronage from successive {{ill|lords of Yoshida Domain|ja|三河吉田藩}}, resulting in several reconstructions. In 1837 (Tenpō 6), it received the highest rank of ''Shoichii'' (Senior First Rank). The shrine’s festivals, influenced by {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭}} in Kyoto, were well-known and recorded in various festival rankings. Following the abolition of domains during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the shrine’s name changed to Yoshida Shrine under the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, and it was reorganized with Susanoo-no-Mikoto as its deity. == Toyohashi Gion Festival == {{main|Toyohashi Gion Festival}} == Precincts == File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Torii.JPG|Entrance Stone Torii File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Altar.JPG|Inner Sanctuary File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Auxiliary Shrines.JPG|Kin Kanmaru and Castle Guardian Inari Shrine File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Fireworks Monument.JPG|Handheld Fireworks Origin Monument File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Handheld Fireworks.JPG|Large Fireworks File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Precinct View.JPG|View from the Precinct File:Toyohashi Gion Matsuri.JPG|Handheld Fireworks Dedication at Toyohashi Gion Festival == Auxiliary Shrines == * Inari Shrine ** Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]] * Izawa Shrine ** Enshrines [[Isasazutome-no-Mikoto]] * Miketsu Shrine ** Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]] and [[Tamatsuhime-no-Mikoto]] == Cultural Properties == [[File:Yoshida Tennō Shrine Festival Painting.jpg|580px|thumb|Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting]] '''Toyohashi City Designated Folk Cultural Properties''' * Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting (including donor inscriptions){{Cite web|date=May 7, 2021|url=http://www.toyohashi-bihaku.jp/?p=14012|title=Yoshida Shrine Items Designated as City Cultural Properties|publisher=Toyohashi City Museum of Art and History|accessdate=April 19, 2022}} : Dimensions: approximately 1.5 m by 3.6 m{{Cite web|date=May 8, 2021|url=http://www.higashiaichi.co.jp/news/detail/7922|title="Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting" Designated as a Folk Cultural Property|publisher=Higashi Aichi Newspaper|accessdate=April 19, 2022}}. Painted by Hatake Achi in 1890 (Meiji 23). The painting depicts festival scenes from the late Edo to early Meiji periods. '''Toyohashi City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties''' * Lion Heads: Created during the Nanboku-chō to Muromachi periods. * Demon Faces: From the Muromachi period. * Mikoshi Ridgepole Plaques: Five plaques dating from 1547 (Tenbun 16) to 1852 (Kaei 5). * Pair of Lions: From the Kamakura period. == Information == ; Address * {{ill|2 Sekiya-cho, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県豊橋市関屋町2番地}} ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|Toyohashi Railroad|ja|豊橋鉄道}}, {{ill|Shiyakusho-mae Station (Aichi)|ja|市役所前停留場}} (approx. 5-minute walk) * Nearest station: {{ill|Meitetsu|ja|名古屋鉄道}}, {{ill|Toyohashi Station|ja|豊橋駅}} (approx. 20-minute walk) == Notes == {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Toyohashi Gion Festival|ja|豊橋祇園祭}} * {{ill|Handheld Fireworks|ja|手筒花火}} * {{ill|Sugimori Hachiman Shrine|ja|杉森八幡社}} * {{ill|Watanabe Koka|ja|渡辺小崋}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoshida-jinja (Toyohashi)}} * [http://toyohashi-yoshida.com Yoshida Shrine Official Website] * [http://toyohashigion.org/index.html Toyohashi Gion Festival Support Association] * [http://www.honokuni.or.jp/toyohashi/spot/000062.html Toyohashi Tourism Convention Association] * [https://www.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/wo06/wo06_3145_56/] Festival Guidebook * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DwAR4KaisJI Yoshida Shrine Toyohashi Gion Festival Handheld Fireworks] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties of Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Gion Shrines]] [[Category:Yasaka Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyohashi City]] [[Category:History of Toyohashi City]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:吉田神社 (豊橋市)]] [[Category:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)]][[Category:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:吉田神社]]

Following the abolition of domains during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the shrine’s name changed to Yoshida Shrine under the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, and it was reorganized with Susanoo-no-Mikoto as its deity. == Toyohashi Gion Festival == {{main|Toyohashi Gion Festival}} == Precincts == File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Torii.JPG|Entrance Stone Torii File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Altar.JPG|Inner Sanctuary File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Auxiliary Shrines.JPG|Kin Kanmaru and Castle Guardian Inari Shrine File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Fireworks Monument.JPG|Handheld Fireworks Origin Monument File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Handheld Fireworks.JPG|Large Fireworks File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Precinct View.JPG|View from the Precinct File:Toyohashi Gion Matsuri.JPG|Handheld Fireworks Dedication at Toyohashi Gion Festival == Auxiliary Shrines == * Inari Shrine ** Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]] * Izawa Shrine ** Enshrines [[Isasazutome-no-Mikoto]] * Miketsu Shrine ** Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]] and [[Tamatsuhime-no-Mikoto]] == Cultural Properties == [[File:Yoshida Tennō Shrine Festival Painting.jpg|580px|thumb|Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting]] '''Toyohashi City Designated Folk Cultural Properties''' * Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting (including donor inscriptions){{Cite web|date=May 7, 2021|url=http://www.toyohashi-bihaku.jp/?p=14012|title=Yoshida Shrine Items Designated as City Cultural Properties|publisher=Toyohashi City Museum of Art and History|accessdate=April 19, 2022}} : Dimensions: approximately 1.5 m by 3.6 m{{Cite web|date=May 8, 2021|url=http://www.higashiaichi.co.jp/news/detail/7922|title="Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting" Designated as a Folk Cultural Property|publisher=Higashi Aichi Newspaper|accessdate=April 19, 2022}}. Painted by Hatake Achi in 1890 (Meiji 23). The painting depicts festival scenes from the late Edo to early Meiji periods. '''Toyohashi City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties''' * Lion Heads: Created during the Nanboku-chō to Muromachi periods. * Demon Faces: From the Muromachi period. * Mikoshi Ridgepole Plaques: Five plaques dating from 1547 (Tenbun 16) to 1852 (Kaei 5). * Pair of Lions: From the Kamakura period. == Information == ; Address * {{ill|2 Sekiya-cho, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県豊橋市関屋町2番地}} ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|Toyohashi Railroad|ja|豊橋鉄道}}, {{ill|Shiyakusho-mae Station (Aichi)|ja|市役所前停留場}} (approx. 5-minute walk) * Nearest station: {{ill|Meitetsu|ja|名古屋鉄道}}, {{ill|Toyohashi Station|ja|豊橋駅}} (approx. 20-minute walk) == Notes == {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Toyohashi Gion Festival|ja|豊橋祇園祭}} * {{ill|Handheld Fireworks|ja|手筒花火}} * {{ill|Sugimori Hachiman Shrine|ja|杉森八幡社}} * {{ill|Watanabe Koka|ja|渡辺小崋}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoshida-jinja (Toyohashi)}} * [http://toyohashi-yoshida.com Yoshida Shrine Official Website] * [http://toyohashigion.org/index.html Toyohashi Gion Festival Support Association] * [http://www.honokuni.or.jp/toyohashi/spot/000062.html Toyohashi Tourism Convention Association] * [https://www.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/wo06/wo06_3145_56/] Festival Guidebook * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DwAR4KaisJI Yoshida Shrine Toyohashi Gion Festival Handheld Fireworks] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties of Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Gion Shrines]] [[Category:Yasaka Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyohashi City]] [[Category:History of Toyohashi City]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:吉田神社 (豊橋市)]] [[Category:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)]][[Category:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:吉田神社]]

{{Shrine |name = Yoshida Shrine |image = [[File:Yoshida shrine.jpg|270px|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) worship hall]]
Worship Hall |address = [[2 Sekiya-cho, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture|愛知県豊橋市関屋町2番地]] |coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-23|display=inline,title}} |deities = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] |rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] |established = (Tradition) [[702 AD]] (Taihō 2) |main_hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] |alias = |annual_festival = [[July]] (third [[Friday]], [[Saturday]], and [[Sunday]]) |rituals = Yoritomo Procession |map = {{Location map|Japan Aichi Prefecture|label=Yoshida Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Yoshida Shrine''' (吉田神社) is a Shinto shrine located at {{ill|Sekiya-cho|ja|関屋町}}, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture. Under the [[Modern Shrine Ranking System|modern shrine ranking system]], it was designated a Prefectural Shrine in {{ill|1921|ja|大正11年}}, having been elevated from a {{ill|village shrine|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == The primary deity enshrined is: * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (素戔嗚尊){{Cite book|title=Rediscovering Our Hometown Guidebook: Learning More About Toyohashi, Vol. 4|date=March 19, 2009|publisher=Toyohashi City Public Relations Division|page=3}} Additionally, [[Empress Jitō]] is enshrined as a complementary deity. == History == [[File:Yoshida, Tenno Festival on the Fifteenth Day of the Sixth Month LACMA M.66.35.23.jpg|thumb|Yoshida Shrine Tennō Festival]] The shrine is said to originate from the temporary palace established during Empress Jitō's visit to Mikawa Province in 702 (Taihō 2). Following her passing, the palace was converted into a shrine dedicated to her. Early records, such as the ''Wakan Sansai Zue,'' refer to it as a "Tennō Shrine enshrining Empress Jitō." During the Edo period, shrine legends record ceremonies honoring her. In 1124 (Tenji 1), during an epidemic, the {{ill|Yokochi clan|ja|横地氏}}, shrine priests, invited [[Gozu Tennō]], the guardian deity of {{ill|Jetavana Monastery|ja|祇園精舎}}, from the {{ill|Kanshin-in|ja|感神院}} (present-day {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}}) to suppress the disease. Subsequently, the shrine was called "Gozu Tennō Shrine" or "Yoshida Tennō Shrine." In 1178 (Jishō 2), during his exile in Izu, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} sent an envoy, Suzuki Jūrō Mototoshi, to pray at Yoshida Shrine. In 1186 (Bunji 2), he dispatched Ishida Jirō Tamehisa for the same purpose. During the Kenkyū era, he ordered Adachi Tōkurō Morinaga, the protector of Mikawa, to rebuild the shrine. In 1507 (Eishō 3), following the construction of {{ill|Yoshida Castle|ja|吉田城 (三河国)}}, {{ill|Makino Kokuhaku|ja|牧野古白}} rebuilt the shrine as the castle’s guardian shrine. In 1548 (Tenbun 16), {{ill|Imagawa Yoshimoto|ja|今川義元}} donated a mikoshi, initiating the shrine’s mikoshi procession. The inscription on the mikoshi’s ridgepole plaque, written by {{ill|Taigen Sessai|ja|太原雪斎}}, reads: “Peace throughout the land, safety of the nation, harmony among the people.” During the Edo period, the shrine received patronage from successive {{ill|lords of Yoshida Domain|ja|三河吉田藩}}, resulting in several reconstructions. In 1837 (Tenpō 6), it received the highest rank of ''Shoichii'' (Senior First Rank). The shrine’s festivals, influenced by {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭}} in Kyoto, were well-known and recorded in various festival rankings. Following the abolition of domains during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the shrine’s name changed to Yoshida Shrine under the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, and it was reorganized with Susanoo-no-Mikoto as its deity. == Toyohashi Gion Festival == {{main|Toyohashi Gion Festival}} == Precincts == File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Torii.JPG|Entrance Stone Torii File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Altar.JPG|Inner Sanctuary File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Auxiliary Shrines.JPG|Kin Kanmaru and Castle Guardian Inari Shrine File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Fireworks Monument.JPG|Handheld Fireworks Origin Monument File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Handheld Fireworks.JPG|Large Fireworks File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Precinct View.JPG|View from the Precinct File:Toyohashi Gion Matsuri.JPG|Handheld Fireworks Dedication at Toyohashi Gion Festival == Auxiliary Shrines == * Inari Shrine ** Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]] * Izawa Shrine ** Enshrines [[Isasazutome-no-Mikoto]] * Miketsu Shrine ** Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]] and [[Tamatsuhime-no-Mikoto]] == Cultural Properties == [[File:Yoshida Tennō Shrine Festival Painting.jpg|580px|thumb|Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting]] '''Toyohashi City Designated Folk Cultural Properties''' * Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting (including donor inscriptions){{Cite web|date=May 7, 2021|url=http://www.toyohashi-bihaku.jp/?p=14012|title=Yoshida Shrine Items Designated as City Cultural Properties|publisher=Toyohashi City Museum of Art and History|accessdate=April 19, 2022}} : Dimensions: approximately 1.5 m by 3.6 m{{Cite web|date=May 8, 2021|url=http://www.higashiaichi.co.jp/news/detail/7922|title="Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting" Designated as a Folk Cultural Property|publisher=Higashi Aichi Newspaper|accessdate=April 19, 2022}}. Painted by Hatake Achi in 1890 (Meiji 23). The painting depicts festival scenes from the late Edo to early Meiji periods. '''Toyohashi City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties''' * Lion Heads: Created during the Nanboku-chō to Muromachi periods. * Demon Faces: From the Muromachi period. * Mikoshi Ridgepole Plaques: Five plaques dating from 1547 (Tenbun 16) to 1852 (Kaei 5). * Pair of Lions: From the Kamakura period. == Information == ; Address * {{ill|2 Sekiya-cho, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県豊橋市関屋町2番地}} ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|Toyohashi Railroad|ja|豊橋鉄道}}, {{ill|Shiyakusho-mae Station (Aichi)|ja|市役所前停留場}} (approx. 5-minute walk) * Nearest station: {{ill|Meitetsu|ja|名古屋鉄道}}, {{ill|Toyohashi Station|ja|豊橋駅}} (approx. 20-minute walk) == Notes == {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Toyohashi Gion Festival|ja|豊橋祇園祭}} * {{ill|Handheld Fireworks|ja|手筒花火}} * {{ill|Sugimori Hachiman Shrine|ja|杉森八幡社}} * {{ill|Watanabe Koka|ja|渡辺小崋}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoshida-jinja (Toyohashi)}} * [http://toyohashi-yoshida.com Yoshida Shrine Official Website] * [http://toyohashigion.org/index.html Toyohashi Gion Festival Support Association] * [http://www.honokuni.or.jp/toyohashi/spot/000062.html Toyohashi Tourism Convention Association] * [https://www.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/wo06/wo06_3145_56/] Festival Guidebook * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DwAR4KaisJI Yoshida Shrine Toyohashi Gion Festival Handheld Fireworks] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties of Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Gion Shrines]] [[Category:Yasaka Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyohashi City]] [[Category:History of Toyohashi City]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{citation needed|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:吉田神社 (豊橋市)]] [[Category:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)]][[Category:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:吉田神社]]

Following the abolition of domains during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the shrine’s name changed to Yoshida Shrine under the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, and it was reorganized with Susanoo-no-Mikoto as its deity. == Toyohashi Gion Festival == {{main|Toyohashi Gion Festival}} == Precincts == File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Torii.JPG|Entrance Stone Torii File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Altar.JPG|Inner Sanctuary File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Auxiliary Shrines.JPG|Kin Kanmaru and Castle Guardian Inari Shrine File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Fireworks Monument.JPG|Handheld Fireworks Origin Monument File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Handheld Fireworks.JPG|Large Fireworks File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Precinct View.JPG|View from the Precinct File:Toyohashi Gion Matsuri.JPG|Handheld Fireworks Dedication at Toyohashi Gion Festival == Auxiliary Shrines == * Inari Shrine ** Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]] * Izawa Shrine ** Enshrines [[Isasazutome-no-Mikoto]] * Miketsu Shrine ** Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]] and [[Tamatsuhime-no-Mikoto]] == Cultural Properties == [[File:Yoshida Tennō Shrine Festival Painting.jpg|580px|thumb|Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting]] '''Toyohashi City Designated Folk Cultural Properties''' * Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting (including donor inscriptions){{Cite web|date=May 7, 2021|url=http://www.toyohashi-bihaku.jp/?p=14012|title=Yoshida Shrine Items Designated as City Cultural Properties|publisher=Toyohashi City Museum of Art and History|accessdate=April 19, 2022}} : Dimensions: approximately 1.5 m by 3.6 m{{Cite web|date=May 8, 2021|url=http://www.higashiaichi.co.jp/news/detail/7922|title="Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting" Designated as a Folk Cultural Property|publisher=Higashi Aichi Newspaper|accessdate=April 19, 2022}}. Painted by Hatake Achi in 1890 (Meiji 23). The painting depicts festival scenes from the late Edo to early Meiji periods. '''Toyohashi City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties''' * Lion Heads: Created during the Nanboku-chō to Muromachi periods. * Demon Faces: From the Muromachi period. * Mikoshi Ridgepole Plaques: Five plaques dating from 1547 (Tenbun 16) to 1852 (Kaei 5). * Pair of Lions: From the Kamakura period. == Information == ; revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Yoshida Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yoshida shrine.jpg|270px|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) worship hall]]
Worship Hall |address = [[2 Sekiya-cho, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture|愛知県豊橋市関屋町2番地]] |coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-23|display=inline,title}} |deities = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] |rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] |established = (Tradition) [[702 AD]] (Taihō 2) |main_hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] |alias = |annual_festival = [[July]] (third [[Friday]], [[Saturday]], and [[Sunday]]) |rituals = Yoritomo Procession |map = {{location map|Japan Aichi Prefecture|label=Yoshida Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Yoshida Shrine''' (吉田神社) is a Shinto shrine located at {{ill|Sekiya-cho|ja|関屋町}}, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture. Under the [[Modern typeing System|modern shrine ranking system]], it was designated a Prefectural Shrine in {{ill|1921|ja|大正11年}}, having been elevated from a {{ill|village shrine|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == The primary deity enshrined is: * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (素戔嗚尊){{Cite book|title=Rediscovering Our Hometown Guidebook: Learning More About Toyohashi, Vol. 4|date=March 19, 2009|publisher=Toyohashi City Public Relations Division|page=3}} Additionally, [[Empress Jitō]] is enshrined as a complementary deity. == History == [[File:Yoshida, Tenno Festival on the Fifteenth Day of the Sixth Month LACMA M.66.35.23.jpg|thumb|Yoshida Shrine Tennō Festival]] The shrine is said to originate from the temporary palace established during Empress Jitō's visit to Mikawa Province in 702 (Taihō 2). Following her passing, the palace was converted into a shrine dedicated to her. Early records, such as the ''Wakan Sansai Zue,'' refer to it as a "Tennō Shrine enshrining Empress Jitō." During the Edo period, shrine legends record ceremonies honoring her. In 1124 (Tenji 1), during an epidemic, the {{ill|Yokochi clan|ja|横地氏}}, shrine priests, invited [[Gozu Tennō]], the guardian deity of {{ill|Jetavana Monastery|ja|祇園精舎}}, from the {{ill|Kanshin-in|ja|感神院}} (present-day {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}}) to suppress the disease. Subsequently, the shrine was called "Gozu Tennō Shrine" or "Yoshida Tennō Shrine." In 1178 (Jishō 2), during his exile in Izu, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} sent an envoy, Suzuki Jūrō Mototoshi, to pray at Yoshida Shrine. In 1186 (Bunji 2), he dispatched Ishida Jirō Tamehisa for the same purpose. During the Kenkyū era, he ordered Adachi Tōkurō Morinaga, the protector of Mikawa, to rebuild the shrine. In 1507 (Eishō 3), following the construction of {{ill|Yoshida Castle|ja|吉田城 (三河国)}}, {{ill|Makino Kokuhaku|ja|牧野古白}} rebuilt the shrine as the castle’s guardian shrine. In 1548 (Tenbun 16), {{ill|Imagawa Yoshimoto|ja|今川義元}} donated a mikoshi, initiating the shrine’s mikoshi procession. The inscription on the mikoshi’s ridgepole plaque, written by {{ill|Taigen Sessai|ja|太原雪斎}}, reads: “Peace throughout the land, safety of the nation, harmony among the people.” During the Edo period, the shrine received patronage from successive {{ill|lords of Yoshida Domain|ja|三河吉田藩}}, resulting in several reconstructions. In 1837 (Tenpō 6), it received the highest rank of ''Shoichii'' (Senior First Rank). The shrine’s festivals, influenced by {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭}} in Kyoto, were well-known and recorded in various festival rankings. Following the abolition of domains during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the shrine’s name changed to Yoshida Shrine under the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, and it was reorganized with Susanoo-no-Mikoto as its deity. == Toyohashi Gion Festival == {{main|Toyohashi Gion Festival}} == Precincts == File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Torii.JPG|Entrance Stone Torii File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Altar.JPG|Inner Sanctuary File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Auxiliary Shrines.JPG|Kin Kanmaru and Castle Guardian Inari Shrine File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Fireworks Monument.JPG|Handheld Fireworks Origin Monument File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Handheld Fireworks.JPG|Large Fireworks File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Precinct View.JPG|View from the Precinct File:Toyohashi Gion Matsuri.JPG|Handheld Fireworks Dedication at Toyohashi Gion Festival == Auxiliary Shrines == * Inari Shrine ** Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]] * Izawa Shrine ** Enshrines [[Isasazutome-no-Mikoto]] * Miketsu Shrine ** Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]] and [[Tamatsuhime-no-Mikoto]] == Cultural Properties == [[File:Yoshida Tennō Shrine Festival Painting.jpg|580px|thumb|Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting]] '''Toyohashi City Designated Folk Cultural Properties''' * Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting (including donor inscriptions){{Cite web|date=May 7, 2021|url=http://www.toyohashi-bihaku.jp/?p=14012|title=Yoshida Shrine Items Designated as City Cultural Properties|publisher=Toyohashi City Museum of Art and History|accessdate=April 19, 2022}} : Dimensions: approximately 1.5 m by 3.6 m{{Cite web|date=May 8, 2021|url=http://www.higashiaichi.co.jp/news/detail/7922|title="Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting" Designated as a Folk Cultural Property|publisher=Higashi Aichi Newspaper|accessdate=April 19, 2022}}. Painted by Hatake Achi in 1890 (Meiji 23). The painting depicts festival scenes from the late Edo to early Meiji periods. '''Toyohashi City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties''' * Lion Heads: Created during the Nanboku-chō to Muromachi periods. * Demon Faces: From the Muromachi period. * Mikoshi Ridgepole Plaques: Five plaques dating from 1547 (Tenbun 16) to 1852 (Kaei 5). * Pair of Lions: From the Kamakura period. == Information == ; Address * {{ill|2 Sekiya-cho, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県豊橋市関屋町2番地}} ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|Toyohashi Railroad|ja|豊橋鉄道}}, {{ill|Shiyakusho-mae Station (Aichi)|ja|市役所前停留場}} (approx. 5-minute walk) * Nearest station: {{ill|Meitetsu|ja|名古屋鉄道}}, {{ill|Toyohashi Station|ja|豊橋駅}} (approx. 20-minute walk) == Notes == {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Toyohashi Gion Festival|ja|豊橋祇園祭}} * {{ill|Handheld Fireworks|ja|手筒花火}} * {{ill|Sugimori Hachiman Shrine|ja|杉森八幡社}} * {{ill|Watanabe Koka|ja|渡辺小崋}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoshida-jinja (Toyohashi)}} * [http://toyohashi-yoshida.com Yoshida Shrine Official Website] * [http://toyohashigion.org/index.html Toyohashi Gion Festival Support Association] * [http://www.honokuni.or.jp/toyohashi/spot/000062.html Toyohashi Tourism Convention Association] * [https://www.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/wo06/wo06_3145_56/] Festival Guidebook * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DwAR4KaisJI Yoshida Shrine Toyohashi Gion Festival Handheld Fireworks] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties of Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Gion Shrines]] [[Category:Yasaka Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyohashi City]] [[Category:History of Toyohashi City]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:吉田神社 (豊橋市)]] [[Category:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)]][[Category:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:吉田神社]]

Following the abolition of domains during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the shrine’s name changed to Yoshida Shrine under the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, and it was reorganized with Susanoo-no-Mikoto as its deity. == Toyohashi Gion Festival == {{main|Toyohashi Gion Festival}} == Precincts == File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Torii.JPG|Entrance Stone Torii File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Altar.JPG|Inner Sanctuary File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Auxiliary Shrines.JPG|Kin Kanmaru and Castle Guardian Inari Shrine File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Fireworks Monument.JPG|Handheld Fireworks Origin Monument File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Handheld Fireworks.JPG|Large Fireworks File:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) Precinct View.JPG|View from the Precinct File:Toyohashi Gion Matsuri.JPG|Handheld Fireworks Dedication at Toyohashi Gion Festival File:吉田神社（豊橋）鳥居.JPG|入口 石鳥居 File:吉田神社（豊橋）祭壇.JPG|内陣 File:吉田神社（豊橋）摂社.JPG|金柑丸・城守護稲荷社 File:吉田神社（豊橋市） - Painted by Hatake Achi in 1890 (Meiji 23). The painting depicts festival scenes from the late Edo to early Meiji periods. '''Toyohashi City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties''' * Lion Heads: Created during the Nanboku-chō to Muromachi periods. * Demon Faces: From the Muromachi period. * Mikoshi Ridgepole Plaques: Five plaques dating from 1547 (Tenbun 16) to 1852 (Kaei 5). * Pair of Lions: From the Kamakura period. == Information == ; Address * {{ill|2 Sekiya-cho, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県豊橋市関屋町2番地}} ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|Toyohashi Railroad|ja|豊橋鉄道}}, {{ill|Shiyakusho-mae Station (Aichi)|ja|市役所前停留場}} (approx. 5-minute walk) * Nearest station: {{ill|Meitetsu|ja|名古屋鉄道}}, {{ill|Toyohashi Station|ja|豊橋駅}} (approx. 20-minute walk) == Notes == {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Toyohashi Gion Festival|ja|豊橋祇園祭}} * {{ill|Handheld Fireworks|ja|手筒花火}} * {{ill|Sugimori Hachiman Shrine|ja|杉森八幡社}} * {{ill|Watanabe Koka|ja|渡辺小崋}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoshida-jinja (Toyohashi)}} * [http://toyohashi-yoshida.com Yoshida Shrine Official Website] * [http://toyohashigion.org/index.html Toyohashi Gion Festival Support Association] * [http://www.honokuni.or.jp/toyohashi/spot/000062.html Toyohashi Tourism Convention Association] * [https://www.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/wo06/wo06_3145_56/] Festival Guidebook * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DwAR4KaisJI Yoshida Shrine Toyohashi Gion Festival Handheld Fireworks] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties of Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Gion Shrines]] [[Category:Yasaka Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyohashi City]] [[Category:History of Toyohashi City]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:吉田神社 (豊橋市)]] [[Category:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)]][[Category:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:吉田神社]]

{{Shrine |name = Yoshida Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yoshida shrine.jpg|270px|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) worship hall]]
Worship Hall |address = [[2 Sekiya-cho, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture|愛知県豊橋市関屋町2番地]] |coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-23|display=inline,title}} |deities = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] |rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] |established = (Tradition) [[702 AD]] (Taihō 2) |main_hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] |alias = |annual_festival = [[July]] (third [[Friday]], [[Saturday]], and [[Sunday]]) |rituals = Yoritomo Procession |map = {{location map|Japan Aichi Prefecture|label=Yoshida Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Yoshida Shrine''' (吉田神社) is a Shinto shrine located at {{ill|Sekiya-cho|ja|関屋町}}, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture. Under the [[Modern typeing System|modern shrine ranking system]], it was designated a Prefectural Shrine in {{ill|1921|ja|大正11年}}, having been elevated from a {{ill|village shrine|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == The primary deity enshrined is: * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (素戔嗚尊){{Cite book|title=Rediscovering Our Hometown Guidebook: Learning More About Toyohashi, Vol. 4|date=March 19, 2009|publisher=Toyohashi City Public Relations Division|page=3}} Additionally, [[Empress Jitō]] is enshrined as a complementary deity. == History == [[File:Yoshida, Tenno Festival on the Fifteenth Day of the Sixth Month LACMA M.66.35.23.jpg|thumb|Yoshida Shrine Tennō Festival]] The shrine is said to originate from the temporary palace established during Empress Jitō's visit to Mikawa Province in 702 (Taihō 2). Following her passing, the palace was converted into a shrine dedicated to her. Early records, such as the ''Wakan Sansai Zue,'' refer to it as a "Tennō Shrine enshrining Empress Jitō." During the Edo period, shrine legends record ceremonies honoring her. In 1124 (Tenji 1), during an epidemic, the {{ill|Yokochi clan|ja|横地氏}}, shrine priests, invited [[Gozu Tennō]], the guardian deity of {{ill|Jetavana Monastery|ja|祇園精舎}}, from the {{ill|Kanshin-in|ja|感神院}} (present-day {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}}) to suppress the disease. Subsequently, the shrine was called "Gozu Tennō Shrine" or "Yoshida Tennō Shrine." In 1178 (Jishō 2), during his exile in Izu, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} sent an envoy, Suzuki Jūrō Mototoshi, to pray at Yoshida Shrine. In 1186 (Bunji 2), he dispatched Ishida Jirō Tamehisa for the same purpose. During the Kenkyū era, he ordered Adachi Tōkurō Morinaga, the protector of Mikawa, to rebuild the shrine. In 1507 (Eishō 3), following the construction of {{ill|Yoshida Castle|ja|吉田城 (三河国)}}, {{ill|Makino Kokuhaku|ja|牧野古白}} rebuilt the shrine as the castle’s guardian shrine. In 1548 (Tenbun 16), {{ill|Imagawa Yoshimoto|ja|今川義元}} donated a mikoshi, initiating the shrine’s mikoshi procession. The inscription on the mikoshi’s ridgepole plaque, written by {{ill|Taigen Sessai|ja|太原雪斎}}, reads: “Peace throughout the land, safety of the nation, harmony among the people.” During the Edo period, the shrine received patronage from successive {{ill|lords of Yoshida Domain|ja|三河吉田藩}}, resulting in several reconstructions. In 1837 (Tenpō 6), it received the highest rank of ''Shoichii'' (Senior First Rank). The shrine’s festivals, influenced by {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭}} in Kyoto, were well-known and recorded in various festival rankings. Following the abolition of domains during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the shrine’s name changed to Yoshida Shrine under the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, and it was reorganized with Susanoo-no-Mikoto as its deity. == Toyohashi Gion Festival == {{main|Toyohashi Gion Festival}} == Precincts == File:吉田神社（豊橋）鳥居.JPG|Entrance Stone Torii File:吉田神社（豊橋）祭壇.JPG|Inner Sanctuary File:吉田神社（豊橋）摂社.JPG|Kin Kanmaru and Castle Guardian Inari Shrine File:吉田神社（豊橋市） - 手筒花火発祥之地 石碑.jpg|Handheld Fireworks Origin Monument File:吉田神社（豊橋市） - 手筒花火1.jpg|Large Fireworks File:吉田神社（豊橋）境内より.JPG|View from the Precinct File:Toyohashi Gion Maturi.JPG|Handheld Fireworks Dedication at Toyohashi Gion Festival == Auxiliary Shrines == * Inari Shrine ** Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]] * Izawa Shrine ** Enshrines [[Isasazutome-no-Mikoto]] * Miketsu Shrine ** Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]] and [[Tamatsuhime-no-Mikoto]] == Cultural Properties == [[File:Yoshida Tennō Shrine Festival Painting.jpg|580px|thumb|Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting]] '''Toyohashi City Designated Folk Cultural Properties''' * Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting (including donor inscriptions){{Cite web|date=May 7, 2021|url=http://www.toyohashi-bihaku.jp/?p=14012|title=Yoshida Shrine Items Designated as City Cultural Properties|publisher=Toyohashi City Museum of Art and History|accessdate=April 19, 2022}} : Dimensions: approximately 1.5 m by 3.6 m{{Cite web|date=May 8, 2021|url=http://www.higashiaichi.co.jp/news/detail/7922|title="Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting" Designated as a Folk Cultural Property|publisher=Higashi Aichi Newspaper|accessdate=April 19, 2022}}. Painted by Hatake Achi in 1890 (Meiji 23). The painting depicts festival scenes from the late Edo to early Meiji periods. '''Toyohashi City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties''' * Lion Heads: Created during the Nanboku-chō to Muromachi periods. * Demon Faces: From the Muromachi period. * Mikoshi Ridgepole Plaques: Five plaques dating from 1547 (Tenbun 16) to 1852 (Kaei 5). * Pair of Lions: From the Kamakura period. == Information == ; Address * {{ill|2 Sekiya-cho, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県豊橋市関屋町2番地}} ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|Toyohashi Railroad|ja|豊橋鉄道}}, {{ill|Shiyakusho-mae Station (Aichi)|ja|市役所前停留場}} (approx. 5-minute walk) * Nearest station: {{ill|Meitetsu|ja|名古屋鉄道}}, {{ill|Toyohashi Station|ja|豊橋駅}} (approx. 20-minute walk) == Notes == {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Toyohashi Gion Festival|ja|豊橋祇園祭}} * {{ill|Handheld Fireworks|ja|手筒花火}} * {{ill|Sugimori Hachiman Shrine|ja|杉森八幡社}} * {{ill|Watanabe Koka|ja|渡辺小崋}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoshida-jinja (Toyohashi)}} * [http://toyohashi-yoshida.com Yoshida Shrine Official Website] * [http://toyohashigion.org/index.html Toyohashi Gion Festival Support Association] * [http://www.honokuni.or.jp/toyohashi/spot/000062.html Toyohashi Tourism Convention Association] * [https://www.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/wo06/wo06_3145_56/] Festival Guidebook * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DwAR4KaisJI Yoshida Shrine Toyohashi Gion Festival Handheld Fireworks] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties of Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Gion Shrines]] [[Category:Yasaka Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyohashi City]] [[Category:History of Toyohashi City]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:吉田神社 (豊橋市)]] [[Category:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)]][[Category:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:吉田神社]]

Following the abolition of domains during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the shrine’s name changed to Yoshida Shrine under the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, and it was reorganized with Susanoo-no-Mikoto as its deity. == Toyohashi Gion Festival == {{main|{{ill|Toyohashi Gion Festival|ja|豊橋祇園祭}}}} == Precincts == File:吉田神社（豊橋）鳥居.JPG|Entrance Stone Torii File:吉田神社（豊橋）祭壇.JPG|Inner Sanctuary File:吉田神社（豊橋）摂社.JPG|Kin Kanmaru and Castle Guardian Inari Shrine File:吉田神社（豊橋市） - 手筒花火発祥之地 石碑.jpg|Handheld Fireworks Origin Monument File:吉田神社（豊橋市） - 手筒花火1.jpg|Large Fireworks File:吉田神社（豊橋）境内より.JPG|View from the Precinct File:Toyohashi Gion Maturi.JPG|Handheld Fireworks Dedication at Toyohashi Gion Festival == Keidai-sha (境内社) == * Inari-sha (稲荷社) ** Saijin: {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|宇迦之魂命}} * Izawa-sha (伊雑社) ** Saijin: {{ill|Isahotomi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊佐波止美命}} * Miketsu-sha (御食社) ** Saijin: {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|宇迦之魂命}}, {{ill|Tamabashiraya-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|玉柱屋比売命}} == Cultural Properties == [[File:Yoshida Tennō Shrine Festival Painting.jpg|580px|thumb|Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting]] '''Toyohashi City Designated Folk Cultural Properties''' * Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting (including donor inscriptions){{Cite web|date=May 7, 2021|url=http://www.toyohashi-bihaku.jp/?p=14012|title=Yoshida Shrine Items Designated as City Cultural Properties|publisher=Toyohashi City Museum of Art and History|accessdate=April 19, 2022}} : Dimensions: approximately 1.5 m by 3.6 m{{Cite web|date=May 8, 2021|url=http://www.higashiaichi.co.jp/news/detail/7922|title="Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting" Designated as a Folk Cultural Property|publisher=Higashi Aichi Newspaper|accessdate=April 19, 2022}}. Painted by Hatake Achi in 1890 (Meiji 23). The painting depicts festival scenes from the late Edo to early Meiji periods. '''Toyohashi City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties''' * Lion Heads: Created during the Nanboku-chō to Muromachi periods. * Demon Faces: From the Muromachi period. * Mikoshi Ridgepole Plaques: Five plaques dating from 1547 (Tenbun 16) to 1852 (Kaei 5). * Pair of Lions: From the Kamakura period. == Information == ; Address * {{ill|2 Sekiya-cho, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県豊橋市関屋町2番地}} ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|Toyohashi Railroad|ja|豊橋鉄道}}, {{ill|Shiyakusho-mae Station (Aichi)|ja|市役所前停留場}} (approx. 5-minute walk) * Nearest station: {{ill|Meitetsu|ja|名古屋鉄道}}, {{ill|Toyohashi Station|ja|豊橋駅}} (approx. 20-minute walk) == Notes == {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Toyohashi Gion Festival|ja|豊橋祇園祭}} * {{ill|Handheld Fireworks|ja|手筒花火}} * {{ill|Sugimori Hachiman Shrine|ja|杉森八幡社}} * {{ill|Watanabe Koka|ja|渡辺小崋}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoshida-jinja (Toyohashi)}} * [http://toyohashi-yoshida.com Yoshida Shrine Official Website] * [http://toyohashigion.org/index.html Toyohashi Gion Festival Support Association] * [http://www.honokuni.or.jp/toyohashi/spot/000062.html Toyohashi Tourism Convention Association] * [https://www.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/wo06/wo06_3145_56/] Festival Guidebook * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DwAR4KaisJI Yoshida Shrine Toyohashi Gion Festival Handheld Fireworks] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties of Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Gion Shrines]] [[Category:Yasaka Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyohashi City]] [[Category:History of Toyohashi City]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:吉田神社 (豊橋市)]] [[Category:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)]][[Category:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:吉田神社]]

{{Shrine |name = Yoshida Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yoshida shrine.jpg|270px|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) worship hall]]
Worship Hall |address = [[2 Sekiya-cho, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture|愛知県豊橋市関屋町2番地]] |coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-23|display=inline,title}} |deities = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] |rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] |established = (Tradition) [[702 AD]] (Taihō 2) |main_hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] |alias = |annual_festival = [[July]] (third [[Friday]], [[Saturday]], and [[Sunday]]) |rituals = Yoritomo Procession |map = {{location map|Japan Aichi Prefecture|label=Yoshida Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Yoshida Shrine''' (吉田神社) is a Shinto shrine located at {{ill|Sekiya-cho|ja|関屋町}}, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture. Under the [[Modern typeing System|modern shrine ranking system]], it was designated a Prefectural Shrine in {{ill|1921|ja|大正11年}}, having been elevated from a {{ill|village shrine|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == The primary deity enshrined is: * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (素戔嗚尊){{Cite book|title=Rediscovering Our Hometown Guidebook: Learning More About Toyohashi, Vol. 4|date=March 19, 2009|publisher=Toyohashi City Public Relations Division|page=3}} Additionally, [[Empress Jitō]] is enshrined as a complementary deity. == History == [[File:Yoshida, Tenno Festival on the Fifteenth Day of the Sixth Month LACMA M.66.35.23.jpg|thumb|Yoshida Shrine Tennō Festival]] The shrine is said to originate from the temporary palace established during Empress Jitō's visit to Mikawa Province in 702 (Taihō 2). Following her passing, the palace was converted into a shrine dedicated to her. Early records, such as the ''Wakan Sansai Zue,'' refer to it as a "Tennō Shrine enshrining Empress Jitō." During the Edo period, shrine legends record ceremonies honoring her. In 1124 (Tenji 1), during an epidemic, the {{ill|Yokochi clan|ja|横地氏}}, shrine priests, invited [[Gozu Tennō]], the guardian deity of {{ill|Jetavana Monastery|ja|祇園精舎}}, from the {{ill|Kanshin-in|ja|感神院}} (present-day {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}}) to suppress the disease. Subsequently, the shrine was called "Gozu Tennō Shrine" or "Yoshida Tennō Shrine." In 1178 (Jishō 2), during his exile in Izu, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} sent an envoy, Suzuki Jūrō Mototoshi, to pray at Yoshida Shrine. In 1186 (Bunji 2), he dispatched Ishida Jirō Tamehisa for the same purpose. During the Kenkyū era, he ordered Adachi Tōkurō Morinaga, the protector of Mikawa, to rebuild the shrine. In 1507 (Eishō 3), following the construction of {{ill|Yoshida Castle|ja|吉田城 (三河国)}}, {{ill|Makino Kokuhaku|ja|牧野古白}} rebuilt the shrine as the castle’s guardian shrine. In 1548 (Tenbun 16), {{ill|Imagawa Yoshimoto|ja|今川義元}} donated a mikoshi, initiating the shrine’s mikoshi procession. The inscription on the mikoshi’s ridgepole plaque, written by {{ill|Taigen Sessai|ja|太原雪斎}}, reads: “Peace throughout the land, safety of the nation, harmony among the people.” During the Edo period, the shrine received patronage from successive {{ill|lords of Yoshida Domain|ja|三河吉田藩}}, resulting in several reconstructions. In 1837 (Tenpō 6), it received the highest rank of ''Shoichii'' (Senior First Rank). The shrine’s festivals, influenced by {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭}} in Kyoto, were well-known and recorded in various festival rankings. Following the abolition of domains during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the shrine’s name changed to Yoshida Shrine under the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, and it was reorganized with Susanoo-no-Mikoto as its deity. == Toyohashi Gion Festival == {{main|{{ill|Toyohashi Gion Festival|ja|豊橋祇園祭}}}} == Precincts == File:吉田神社（豊橋）鳥居.JPG|Entrance Stone Torii File:吉田神社（豊橋）祭壇.JPG|Inner Sanctuary File:吉田神社（豊橋）摂社.JPG|Kin Kanmaru and Castle Guardian Inari Shrine File:吉田神社（豊橋市） - 手筒花火発祥之地 石碑.jpg|Handheld Fireworks Origin Monument File:吉田神社（豊橋市） - 手筒花火1.jpg|Large Fireworks File:吉田神社（豊橋）境内より.JPG|View from the Precinct File:Toyohashi Gion Maturi.JPG|Handheld Fireworks Dedication at Toyohashi Gion Festival == Keidai-sha (境内社) == * Inari-sha (稲荷社) ** Saijin: {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|宇迦之魂命}} * Izawa-sha (伊雑社) ** Saijin: {{ill|Isahotomi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊佐波止美命}} * Miketsu-sha (御食社) ** Saijin: {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|宇迦之魂命}}, {{ill|Tamabashiraya-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|玉柱屋比売命}} == Cultural Properties == [[File:吉田天王社祭礼図.jpg|thumb|580x580px|Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting]] '''Toyohashi City Designated Folk Cultural Properties''' * Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting (including donor inscriptions){{Cite web|date=May 7, 2021|url=http://www.toyohashi-bihaku.jp/?p=14012|title=Yoshida Shrine Items Designated as City Cultural Properties|publisher=Toyohashi City Museum of Art and History|accessdate=April 19, 2022}} : Dimensions: approximately 1.5 m by 3.6 m{{Cite web|date=May 8, 2021|url=http://www.higashiaichi.co.jp/news/detail/7922|title="Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting" Designated as a Folk Cultural Property|publisher=Higashi Aichi Newspaper|accessdate=April 19, 2022}}. Painted by Hatake Achi in 1890 (Meiji 23). The painting depicts festival scenes from the late Edo to early Meiji periods. '''Toyohashi City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties''' * Lion Heads: Created during the Nanboku-chō to Muromachi periods. * Demon Faces: From the Muromachi period. * Mikoshi Ridgepole Plaques: Five plaques dating from 1547 (Tenbun 16) to 1852 (Kaei 5). * Pair of Lions: From the Kamakura period. == Information == ; Address * {{ill|2 Sekiya-cho, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県豊橋市関屋町2番地}} ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|Toyohashi Railroad|ja|豊橋鉄道}}, {{ill|Shiyakusho-mae Station (Aichi)|ja|市役所前停留場}} (approx. 5-minute walk) * Nearest station: {{ill|Meitetsu|ja|名古屋鉄道}}, {{ill|Toyohashi Station|ja|豊橋駅}} (approx. 20-minute walk) == Notes == {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Toyohashi Gion Festival|ja|豊橋祇園祭}} * {{ill|Handheld Fireworks|ja|手筒花火}} * {{ill|Sugimori Hachiman Shrine|ja|杉森八幡社}} * {{ill|Watanabe Koka|ja|渡辺小崋}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoshida-jinja (Toyohashi)}} * [http://toyohashi-yoshida.com Yoshida Shrine Official Website] * [http://toyohashigion.org/index.html Toyohashi Gion Festival Support Association] * [http://www.honokuni.or.jp/toyohashi/spot/000062.html Toyohashi Tourism Convention Association] * [https://www.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/wo06/wo06_3145_56/] Festival Guidebook * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DwAR4KaisJI Yoshida Shrine Toyohashi Gion Festival Handheld Fireworks] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties of Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Gion Shrines]] [[Category:Yasaka Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyohashi City]] [[Category:History of Toyohashi City]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:吉田神社 (豊橋市)]] [[Category:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)]][[Category:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:吉田神社]]

In 1837 (Tenpō 6), it received the highest rank of ''Shoichii'' (Senior First Rank). The shrine’s festivals, influenced by {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭}} in Kyoto, were well-known and recorded in various festival rankings. Following the abolition of domains during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the shrine’s name changed to Yoshida Shrine under the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, and it was reorganized with Susanoo-no-Mikoto as its deity. == Toyohashi Gion Festival == {{main|{{ill|Toyohashi Gion Festival|ja|豊橋祇園祭}}}} == Precincts == File:吉田神社（豊橋）鳥居.JPG|Entrance Stone Torii File:吉田神社（豊橋）祭壇.JPG|Inner Sanctuary File:吉田神社（豊橋）摂社.JPG|Kin Kanmaru and Castle Guardian Inari Shrine File:吉田神社（豊橋市） - 手筒花火発祥之地 石碑.jpg|Handheld Fireworks Origin Monument File:吉田神社（豊橋市） - 手筒花火1.jpg|Large Fireworks File:吉田神社（豊橋）境内より.JPG|View from the Precinct File:Toyohashi Gion Maturi.JPG|Handheld Fireworks Dedication at Toyohashi Gion Festival == Keidai-sha (境内社) == * Inari-sha (稲荷社) ** Saijin: {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|宇迦之魂命}} * Izawa-sha (伊雑社) ** Saijin: {{ill|Isahotomi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊佐波止美命}} * Miketsu-sha (御食社) ** Saijin: {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|宇迦之魂命}}, {{ill|Tamabashiraya-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|玉柱屋比売命}} == Cultural Properties == [[File:吉田天王社祭礼図.jpg|thumb|580x580px|Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting]] '''Toyohashi City Designated Folk Cultural Properties''' * Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting (including donor inscriptions){{Cite web|date=May 7, 2021|url=http://www.toyohashi-bihaku.jp/?p=14012|title=Yoshida Shrine Items Designated as City Cultural Properties|publisher=Toyohashi City Museum of Art and History|accessdate=April 19, 2022}} : Dimensions: approximately 1.5 m by 3.6 m{{Cite web|date=May 8, 2021|url=http://www.higashiaichi.co.jp/news/detail/7922|title="Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting" Designated as a Folk Cultural Property|publisher=Higashi Aichi Newspaper|accessdate=April 19, 2022}}. Painted by Hatake Achi in 1890 (Meiji 23). The painting depicts festival scenes from the late Edo to early Meiji periods. '''Toyohashi City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties''' * Lion Heads: Created during the Nanboku-chō to Muromachi periods. * Demon Faces: From the Muromachi period. * Mikoshi Ridgepole Plaques: Five plaques dating from 1547 (Tenbun 16) to 1852 (Kaei 5). * Pair of Lions: From the Kamakura period. == Information == ; Address * {{ill|2 Sekiya-cho, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県豊橋市関屋町2番地}} ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|Toyohashi Railroad|ja|豊橋鉄道}}, {{ill|Shiyakusho-mae Station (Aichi)|ja|市役所前停留場}} (approx. 5-minute walk) * Nearest station: {{ill|Meitetsu|ja|名古屋鉄道}}, {{ill|Toyohashi Station|ja|豊橋駅}} (approx. 20-minute walk) == Notes == {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Toyohashi Gion Festival|ja|豊橋祇園祭}} * [[:en:Tezutsu-hanabi|Tezutsu-hanabi]] * {{ill|Sugimori Hachiman Shrine|ja|杉森八幡社}} * {{ill|Watanabe Koka|ja|渡辺小崋}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoshida-jinja (Toyohashi)}} * [http://toyohashi-yoshida.com Yoshida Shrine Official Website] * [http://toyohashigion.org/index.html Toyohashi Gion Festival Support Association] * [http://www.honokuni.or.jp/toyohashi/spot/000062.html Toyohashi Tourism Convention Association] * [https://www.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/wo06/wo06_3145_56/] Festival Guidebook * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DwAR4KaisJI Yoshida Shrine Toyohashi Gion Festival Handheld Fireworks] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties of Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Gion Shrines]] [[Category:Yasaka Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyohashi City]] [[Category:History of Toyohashi City]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:吉田神社 (豊橋市)]] [[Category:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)]][[Category:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)]] [[ja:吉田神社]]

{{Shrine |name = Yoshida Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yoshida shrine.jpg|270px|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) worship hall]]
Worship Hall |address = [[2 Sekiya-cho, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture|愛知県豊橋市関屋町2番地]] |coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-23|display=inline,title}} |deities = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] |rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] |established = (Tradition) [[702 AD]] (Taihō 2) |main_hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] |alias = |annual_festival = [[July]] (third [[Friday]], [[Saturday]], and [[Sunday]]) |rituals = Yoritomo Procession |map = {{location map|Japan Aichi Prefecture|label=Yoshida Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Yoshida Shrine''' (吉田神社) is a Shinto shrine located at {{ill|Sekiya-cho|ja|関屋町}}, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture. Under the [[Modern typeing System|modern shrine ranking system]], it was designated a Prefectural Shrine in {{ill|1921|ja|大正11年}}, having been elevated from a {{ill|village shrine|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == The primary deity enshrined is: * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (素戔嗚尊){{Cite book|title=Rediscovering Our Hometown Guidebook: Learning More About Toyohashi, Vol. 4|date=March 19, 2009|publisher=Toyohashi City Public Relations Division|page=3}} Additionally, [[Empress Jitō]] is enshrined as a complementary deity. == History == [[File:Yoshida, Tenno Festival on the Fifteenth Day of the Sixth Month LACMA M.66.35.23.jpg|thumb|Yoshida Shrine Tennō Festival]] The shrine is said to originate from the temporary palace established during Empress Jitō's visit to Mikawa Province in 702 (Taihō 2). Following her passing, the palace was converted into a shrine dedicated to her. Early records, such as the ''Wakan Sansai Zue,'' refer to it as a "Tennō Shrine enshrining Empress Jitō." During the Edo period, shrine legends record ceremonies honoring her. In 1124 (Tenji 1), during an epidemic, the {{ill|Yokochi clan|ja|横地氏}}, shrine priests, invited [[Gozu Tennō]], the guardian deity of {{ill|Jetavana Monastery|ja|祇園精舎}}, from the {{ill|Kanshin-in|ja|感神院}} (present-day {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}}) to suppress the disease. Subsequently, the shrine was called "Gozu Tennō Shrine" or "Yoshida Tennō Shrine." In 1178 (Jishō 2), during his exile in Izu, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} sent an envoy, Suzuki Jūrō Mototoshi, to pray at Yoshida Shrine. In 1186 (Bunji 2), he dispatched Ishida Jirō Tamehisa for the same purpose. During the Kenkyū era, he ordered Adachi Tōkurō Morinaga, the protector of Mikawa, to rebuild the shrine. In 1507 (Eishō 3), following the construction of {{ill|Yoshida Castle|ja|吉田城 (三河国)}}, {{ill|Makino Kokuhaku|ja|牧野古白}} rebuilt the shrine as the castle’s guardian shrine. In 1548 (Tenbun 16), {{ill|Imagawa Yoshimoto|ja|今川義元}} donated a mikoshi, initiating the shrine’s mikoshi procession. The inscription on the mikoshi’s ridgepole plaque, written by {{ill|Taigen Sessai|ja|太原雪斎}}, reads: “Peace throughout the land, safety of the nation, harmony among the people.” During the Edo period, the shrine received patronage from successive {{ill|lords of Yoshida Domain|ja|三河吉田藩}}, resulting in several reconstructions. In 1837 (Tenpō 6), it received the highest rank of ''Shoichii'' (Senior First Rank). The shrine’s festivals, influenced by {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭}} in Kyoto, were well-known and recorded in various festival rankings. Following the abolition of domains during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the shrine’s name changed to Yoshida Shrine under the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, and it was reorganized with Susanoo-no-Mikoto as its deity. == Toyohashi Gion Festival == {{main|{{ill|Toyohashi Gion Festival|ja|豊橋祇園祭}}}} == Precincts == File:吉田神社（豊橋）鳥居.JPG|Entrance Stone Torii File:吉田神社（豊橋）祭壇.JPG|Inner Sanctuary File:吉田神社（豊橋）摂社.JPG|Kin Kanmaru and Castle Guardian Inari Shrine File:吉田神社（豊橋市） - 手筒花火発祥之地 石碑.jpg|Handheld Fireworks Origin Monument File:吉田神社（豊橋市） - 手筒花火1.jpg|Large Fireworks File:吉田神社（豊橋）境内より.JPG|View from the Precinct File:Toyohashi Gion Maturi.JPG|Handheld Fireworks Dedication at Toyohashi Gion Festival == Keidai-sha (境内社) == * Inari-sha (稲荷社) ** Saijin: {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|宇迦之魂命}} * Izawa-sha (伊雑社) ** Saijin: {{ill|Izawa-no-miya|lt=Isahotomi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊佐波止美命}} * Miketsu-sha (御食社) ** Saijin: {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|宇迦之魂命}}, {{ill|Izawa-no-miya|lt=Tamabashiraya-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|玉柱屋比売命}} == Cultural Properties == [[File:吉田天王社祭礼図.jpg|thumb|580x580px|Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting]] '''Toyohashi City Designated Folk Cultural Properties''' * Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting (including donor inscriptions){{Cite web|date=May 7, 2021|url=http://www.toyohashi-bihaku.jp/?p=14012|title=Yoshida Shrine Items Designated as City Cultural Properties|publisher=Toyohashi City Museum of Art and History|accessdate=April 19, 2022}} : Dimensions: approximately 1.5 m by 3.6 m{{Cite web|date=May 8, 2021|url=http://www.higashiaichi.co.jp/news/detail/7922|title="Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting" Designated as a Folk Cultural Property|publisher=Higashi Aichi Newspaper|accessdate=April 19, 2022}}. Painted by Hatake Achi in 1890 (Meiji 23). The painting depicts festival scenes from the late Edo to early Meiji periods. '''Toyohashi City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties''' * Lion Heads: Created during the Nanboku-chō to Muromachi periods. * Demon Faces: From the Muromachi period. * Mikoshi Ridgepole Plaques: Five plaques dating from 1547 (Tenbun 16) to 1852 (Kaei 5). * Pair of Lions: From the Kamakura period. == Information == ; Address * {{ill|2 Sekiya-cho, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県豊橋市関屋町2番地}} ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|Toyohashi Railroad|ja|豊橋鉄道}}, {{ill|Shiyakusho-mae Station (Aichi)|ja|市役所前停留場}} (approx. 5-minute walk) * Nearest station: {{ill|Meitetsu|ja|名古屋鉄道}}, {{ill|Toyohashi Station|ja|豊橋駅}} (approx. 20-minute walk) == Notes == {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Toyohashi Gion Festival|ja|豊橋祇園祭}} * [[:en:Tezutsu-hanabi|Tezutsu-hanabi]] * {{ill|Sugimori Hachiman Shrine|ja|杉森八幡社}} * {{ill|Watanabe Koka|ja|渡辺小崋}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoshida-jinja (Toyohashi)}} * [http://toyohashi-yoshida.com Yoshida Shrine Official Website] * [http://toyohashigion.org/index.html Toyohashi Gion Festival Support Association] * [http://www.honokuni.or.jp/toyohashi/spot/000062.html Toyohashi Tourism Convention Association] * [https://www.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/wo06/wo06_3145_56/] Festival Guidebook * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DwAR4KaisJI Yoshida Shrine Toyohashi Gion Festival Handheld Fireworks] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties of Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Gion Shrines]] [[Category:Yasaka Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyohashi City]] [[Category:History of Toyohashi City]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:吉田神社 (豊橋市)]] [[Category:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)]][[Category:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)]] [[ja:吉田神社]]
