{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yoshida Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yoshida shrine.jpg|270px|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) worship hall]]
Worship Hall |address = [[2 Sekiya-cho, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture|愛知県豊橋市関屋町2番地]] |coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-23|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] |Rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] |established = (Tradition) [[:en:702 AD|702 AD]] (Taihō 2) |main_hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] |alias = |annual_festival = [[July]] (third [[Friday]], [[Saturday]], and [[Sunday]]) |rituals = Yoritomo Procession |map = {{location map|Japan Aichi Prefecture|label=Yoshida Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Yoshida Shrine''' (吉田神社) is a Shinto shrine located at {{ill|Sekiya-cho|ja|関屋町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekiya-cho|qq=}}, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture. Under the [[Modern typeing System|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was designated a Prefectural Shrine in {{ill|1921|ja|大正11年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1921|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1922年}}, having been elevated from a {{ill|village shrine|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Deities == The primary deity enshrined is: * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (素戔嗚尊){{Cite book|title=Rediscovering Our Hometown Guidebook: Learning More About Toyohashi, Vol. 4|date=March 19, 2009|publisher=Toyohashi City Public Relations Division|page=3}} Additionally, [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] is enshrined as a complementary deity. == History == [[File:Yoshida, Tenno Festival on the Fifteenth Day of the Sixth Month LACMA M.66.35.23.jpg|thumb|Yoshida Shrine Tennō Festival]] The shrine is said to originate from the temporary palace established during Empress Jitō's visit to Mikawa Province in 702 (Taihō 2). Following her passing, the palace was converted into a shrine dedicated to her. Early records, such as the ''Wakan Sansai Zue,'' refer to it as a "Tennō Shrine enshrining Empress Jitō." During the Edo period, shrine legends record ceremonies honoring her. In 1124 (Tenji 1), during an epidemic, the {{ill|Yokochi clan|ja|横地氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokochi clan|qq=}}, shrine priests, invited [[Gozu Tennō]], the guardian deity of [[:en:Jetavana|Jetavana monastery]], from the {{ill|Kanshin-in|ja|感神院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanshin-in|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園信仰}} (present-day [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]]) to suppress the disease. Subsequently, the shrine was called "Gozu Tennō Shrine" or "Yoshida Tennō Shrine." In 1178 (Jishō 2), during his exile in Izu, [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] sent an envoy, Suzuki Jūrō Mototoshi, to pray at Yoshida Shrine. In 1186 (Bunji 2), he dispatched Ishida Jirō Tamehisa for the same purpose. During the Kenkyū era, he ordered Adachi Tōkurō Morinaga, the protector of Mikawa, to rebuild the shrine. In 1507 (Eishō 3), following the construction of [[:en:Yoshida Castle (Mikawa Province)|Yoshida Castle]], {{ill|Makino Kokuhaku|ja|牧野古白|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makino Kokuhaku|qq=}} rebuilt the shrine as the castle’s guardian shrine. In 1548 (Tenbun 16), [[:en:Imagawa Yoshimoto|Imagawa Yoshimoto]] donated a mikoshi, initiating the shrine’s mikoshi procession. The inscription on the mikoshi’s ridgepole plaque, written by [[:en:Sessai Chōrō|Taigen Sessai]], reads: “Peace throughout the land, safety of the nation, harmony among the people.” During the Edo period, the shrine received patronage from successive [[:en:Yoshida Domain|lords of Yoshida Domain]], resulting in several reconstructions. In 1837 (Tenpō 6), it received the highest Rank of ''Shoichii'' (Senior First Rank). The shrine’s festivals, influenced by [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]] in Kyoto, were well-known and recorded in various festival Rankings. Following the abolition of domains during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the shrine’s name changed to Yoshida Shrine under the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}}, and it was reorganized with Susanoo-no-Mikoto as its deity. == Toyohashi Gion Festival == {{main|{{ill|Toyohashi Gion Festival|ja|豊橋祇園祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohashi Gion Festival|qq=}}}} == Precincts == File:吉田神社（豊橋）鳥居.JPG|Entrance Stone Torii File:吉田神社（豊橋）祭壇.JPG|Inner Sanctuary File:吉田神社（豊橋）摂社.JPG|Kin Kanmaru and Castle Guardian Inari Shrine File:吉田神社（豊橋市） - 手筒花火発祥之地 石碑.jpg|Handheld Fireworks Origin Monument File:吉田神社（豊橋市） - 手筒花火1.jpg|Large Fireworks File:吉田神社（豊橋）境内より.JPG|View from the Precinct File:Toyohashi Gion Maturi.JPG|Handheld Fireworks Dedication at Toyohashi Gion Festival == Keidai-sha (境内社) == * Inari-sha (稲荷社) ** Saijin: {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|宇迦之魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} * Izawa-sha (伊雑社) ** Saijin: {{ill|Izawa-no-miya|lt=Isahotomi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊佐波止美命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izawa-no-miya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊雑宮}} * Miketsu-sha (御食社) ** Saijin: {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|宇迦之魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, {{ill|Izawa-no-miya|lt=Tamabashiraya-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|玉柱屋比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izawa-no-miya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊雑宮}} == Cultural Properties == [[File:吉田天王社祭礼図.jpg|thumb|580x580px|Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting]] '''Toyohashi City Designated Folk Cultural Properties''' * Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting (including donor inscriptions){{Cite web|date=May 7, 2021|url=http://www.toyohashi-bihaku.jp/?p=14012|title=Yoshida Shrine Items Designated as City Cultural Properties|publisher=Toyohashi City Museum of Art and History|accessdate=April 19, 2022}} : Dimensions: approximately 1.5 m by 3.6 m{{Cite web|date=May 8, 2021|url=http://www.higashiaichi.co.jp/news/detail/7922|title="Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting" Designated as a Folk Cultural Property|publisher=Higashi Aichi Newspaper|accessdate=April 19, 2022}}. Painted by Hatake Achi in 1890 (Meiji 23). The painting depicts festival scenes from the late Edo to early Meiji periods. '''Toyohashi City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties''' * Lion Heads: Created during the Nanboku-chō to Muromachi periods. * Demon Faces: From the Muromachi period. * Mikoshi Ridgepole Plaques: Five plaques dating from 1547 (Tenbun 16) to 1852 (Kaei 5). * Pair of Lions: From the Kamakura period. == Information == ; Address * {{ill|2 Sekiya-cho, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県豊橋市関屋町2番地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2 Sekiya-cho, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; Access * Nearest station: [[:en:Toyohashi Railroad|Toyohashi Railroad]], {{ill|Shiyakusho-mae Station (Aichi)|ja|市役所前停留場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiyakusho-mae Station (Aichi)|qq=}} (approx. 5-minute walk) * Nearest station: [[:en:Meitetsu|Meitetsu]], [[:en:Toyohashi Station|Toyohashi Station]] (approx. 20-minute walk) == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Toyohashi Gion Festival|ja|豊橋祇園祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohashi Gion Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:Tezutsu-hanabi|Tezutsu-hanabi]] * {{ill|Sugimori Hachiman Shrine|ja|杉森八幡社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugimori Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Watanabe Koka|ja|渡辺小崋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watanabe Koka|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoshida-jinja (Toyohashi)}} * [http://toyohashi-yoshida.com Yoshida Shrine Official Website] * [http://toyohashigion.org/index.html Toyohashi Gion Festival Support Association] * [http://www.honokuni.or.jp/toyohashi/spot/000062.html Toyohashi Tourism Convention Association] * [https://www.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/wo06/wo06_3145_56/] Festival Guidebook * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DwAR4KaisJI Yoshida Shrine Toyohashi Gion Festival Handheld Fireworks] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|吉田神社 (豊橋市)|version=101623112|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Yasaka Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)]] [[ja:吉田神社 (豊橋市)]] [[ja:吉田神社]] [[Category:Yasaka-jinja]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:愛知県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Toyohashi]] [[Category:History of Toyohashi, Aichi]] {{Commons category|Yoshida-jinja (Toyohashi)}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yoshida Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yoshida shrine.jpg|270px|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) worship hall]]
Worship Hall |address = [[2 Sekiya-cho, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture|愛知県豊橋市関屋町2番地]] |coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-23|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] |Rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] |established = (Tradition) [[:en:702 AD|702 AD]] (Taihō 2) |main_hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] |alias = |annual_festival = [[July]] (third [[Friday]], [[Saturday]], and [[Sunday]]) |rituals = Yoritomo Procession |map = {{location map|Japan Aichi Prefecture|label=Yoshida Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Yoshida Shrine''' (吉田神社) is a Shinto shrine located at {{ill|Sekiya-cho|ja|関屋町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekiya-cho|qq=}}, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture. Under the [[Modern typeing System|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was designated a Prefectural Shrine in {{ill|1921|ja|大正11年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1921|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1922年}}, having been elevated from a {{ill|village shrine|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Deities == The primary deity enshrined is: * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (素戔嗚尊){{Cite book|title=Rediscovering Our Hometown Guidebook: Learning More About Toyohashi, Vol. 4|date=March 19, 2009|publisher=Toyohashi City Public Relations Division|page=3}} Additionally, [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] is enshrined as a complementary deity. == History == [[File:Yoshida, Tenno Festival on the Fifteenth Day of the Sixth Month LACMA M.66.35.23.jpg|thumb|Yoshida Shrine Tennō Festival]] The shrine is said to originate from the temporary palace established during Empress Jitō's visit to Mikawa Province in 702 (Taihō 2). Following her passing, the palace was converted into a shrine dedicated to her. Early records, such as the ''Wakan Sansai Zue,'' refer to it as a "Tennō Shrine enshrining Empress Jitō." During the Edo period, shrine legends record ceremonies honoring her. In 1124 (Tenji 1), during an epidemic, the {{ill|Yokochi clan|ja|横地氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokochi clan|qq=}}, shrine priests, invited [[Gozu Tennō]], the guardian deity of [[:en:Jetavana|Jetavana monastery]], from the {{ill|Kanshin-in|ja|感神院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanshin-in|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園信仰}} (present-day [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]]) to suppress the disease. Subsequently, the shrine was called "Gozu Tennō Shrine" or "Yoshida Tennō Shrine." In 1178 (Jishō 2), during his exile in Izu, [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] sent an envoy, Suzuki Jūrō Mototoshi, to pray at Yoshida Shrine. In 1186 (Bunji 2), he dispatched Ishida Jirō Tamehisa for the same purpose. During the Kenkyū era, he ordered Adachi Tōkurō Morinaga, the protector of Mikawa, to rebuild the shrine. In 1507 (Eishō 3), following the construction of [[:en:Yoshida Castle (Mikawa Province)|Yoshida Castle]], {{ill|Makino Kokuhaku|ja|牧野古白|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makino Kokuhaku|qq=}} rebuilt the shrine as the castle’s guardian shrine. In 1548 (Tenbun 16), [[:en:Imagawa Yoshimoto|Imagawa Yoshimoto]] donated a mikoshi, initiating the shrine’s mikoshi procession. The inscription on the mikoshi’s ridgepole plaque, written by [[:en:Sessai Chōrō|Taigen Sessai]], reads: “Peace throughout the land, safety of the nation, harmony among the people.” During the Edo period, the shrine received patronage from successive [[:en:Yoshida Domain|lords of Yoshida Domain]], resulting in several reconstructions. In 1837 (Tenpō 6), it received the highest Rank of ''Shoichii'' (Senior First Rank). The shrine’s festivals, influenced by [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]] in Kyoto, were well-known and recorded in various festival Rankings. Following the abolition of domains during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the shrine’s name changed to Yoshida Shrine under the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}}, and it was reorganized with Susanoo-no-Mikoto as its deity. == Toyohashi Gion Festival == {{main|{{ill|Toyohashi Gion Festival|ja|豊橋祇園祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohashi Gion Festival|qq=}}}} == Precincts == File:吉田神社（豊橋）鳥居.JPG|Entrance Stone Torii File:吉田神社（豊橋）祭壇.JPG|Inner Sanctuary File:吉田神社（豊橋）摂社.JPG|Kin Kanmaru and Castle Guardian Inari Shrine File:吉田神社（豊橋市） - 手筒花火発祥之地 石碑.jpg|Handheld Fireworks Origin Monument File:吉田神社（豊橋市） - 手筒花火1.jpg|Large Fireworks File:吉田神社（豊橋）境内より.JPG|View from the Precinct File:Toyohashi Gion Maturi.JPG|Handheld Fireworks Dedication at Toyohashi Gion Festival == Keidai-sha (境内社) == * Inari-sha (稲荷社) ** Saijin: {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|宇迦之魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} * Izawa-sha (伊雑社) ** Saijin: {{ill|Izawa-no-miya|lt=Isahotomi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊佐波止美命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izawa-no-miya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊雑宮}} * Miketsu-sha (御食社) ** Saijin: {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|宇迦之魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, {{ill|Izawa-no-miya|lt=Tamabashiraya-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|玉柱屋比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izawa-no-miya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊雑宮}} == Cultural Properties == [[File:吉田天王社祭礼図.jpg|thumb|580x580px|Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting]] '''Toyohashi City Designated Folk Cultural Properties''' * Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting (including donor inscriptions){{Cite web|date=May 7, 2021|url=http://www.toyohashi-bihaku.jp/?p=14012|title=Yoshida Shrine Items Designated as City Cultural Properties|publisher=Toyohashi City Museum of Art and History|accessdate=April 19, 2022}} : Dimensions: approximately 1.5 m by 3.6 m{{Cite web|date=May 8, 2021|url=http://www.higashiaichi.co.jp/news/detail/7922|title="Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting" Designated as a Folk Cultural Property|publisher=Higashi Aichi Newspaper|accessdate=April 19, 2022}}. Painted by Hatake Achi in 1890 (Meiji 23). The painting depicts festival scenes from the late Edo to early Meiji periods. '''Toyohashi City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties''' * Lion Heads: Created during the Nanboku-chō to Muromachi periods. * Demon Faces: From the Muromachi period. * Mikoshi Ridgepole Plaques: Five plaques dating from 1547 (Tenbun 16) to 1852 (Kaei 5). * Pair of Lions: From the Kamakura period. == Information == ; Address * {{ill|2 Sekiya-cho, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県豊橋市関屋町2番地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2 Sekiya-cho, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; Access * Nearest station: [[:en:Toyohashi Railroad|Toyohashi Railroad]], {{ill|Shiyakusho-mae Station (Aichi)|ja|市役所前停留場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiyakusho-mae Station (Aichi)|qq=}} (approx. 5-minute walk) * Nearest station: [[:en:Meitetsu|Meitetsu]], [[:en:Toyohashi Station|Toyohashi Station]] (approx. 20-minute walk) == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Toyohashi Gion Festival|ja|豊橋祇園祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohashi Gion Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:Tezutsu-hanabi|Tezutsu-hanabi]] * {{ill|Sugimori Hachiman Shrine|ja|杉森八幡社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugimori Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Watanabe Koka|ja|渡辺小崋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watanabe Koka|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoshida-jinja (Toyohashi)}} * [http://toyohashi-yoshida.com Yoshida Shrine Official Website] * [http://toyohashigion.org/index.html Toyohashi Gion Festival Support Association] * [http://www.honokuni.or.jp/toyohashi/spot/000062.html Toyohashi Tourism Convention Association] * [https://www.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/wo06/wo06_3145_56/] Festival Guidebook * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DwAR4KaisJI Yoshida Shrine Toyohashi Gion Festival Handheld Fireworks] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|吉田神社 (豊橋市)|version=101623112|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)]] [[ja:吉田神社 (豊橋市)]] [[ja:吉田神社]] [[Category:Yasaka-jinja]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:愛知県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Toyohashi]] [[Category:History of Toyohashi, Aichi]] {{Commons category|Yoshida-jinja (Toyohashi)}}

Subsequently, the shrine was called "Gozu Tennō Shrine" or "Yoshida Tennō Shrine." The shrine’s festivals, influenced by [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]] in Kyoto, were well-known and recorded in various festival Rankings. 手筒花火発祥之地 石碑.jpg|Handheld Fireworks Origin Monument File:吉田神社（豊橋市） - 手筒花火1.jpg|Large Fireworks File:吉田神社（豊橋）境内より.JPG|View from the Precinct File:Toyohashi Gion Maturi.JPG|Handheld Fireworks Dedication at Toyohashi Gion Festival == Keidai-sha (境内社) == * Inari-sha (稲荷社) ** Saijin: {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|宇迦之魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} * Izawa-sha (伊雑社) ** Saijin: {{ill|Izawa-no-miya|lt=Isahotomi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊佐波止美命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izawa-no-miya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊雑宮}} * Miketsu-sha (御食社) ** Saijin: {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|宇迦之魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, {{ill|Izawa-no-miya|lt=Tamabashiraya-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|玉柱屋比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izawa-no-miya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊雑宮}} == Cultural Properties == [[File:吉田天王社祭礼図.jpg|thumb|580x580px|Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting]] '''Toyohashi City Designated Folk Cultural Properties''' * Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting (including donor inscriptions){{Cite web|date=May 7, 2021|url=http://www.toyohashi-bihaku.jp/?p=14012|title=Yoshida Shrine Items Designated as City Cultural Properties|publisher=Toyohashi City Museum of Art and History|accessdate=April 19, 2022}} : Dimensions: approximately 1.5 m by 3.6 m{{Cite web|date=May 8, 2021|url=http://www.higashiaichi.co.jp/news/detail/7922|title="Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting" Designated as a Folk Cultural Property|publisher=Higashi Aichi Newspaper|accessdate=April 19, 2022}}. Painted by Hatake Achi in 1890 (Meiji 23). The painting depicts festival scenes from the late Edo to early Meiji periods. '''Toyohashi City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties''' * Lion Heads: Created during the Nanboku-chō to Muromachi periods. * Demon Faces: From the Muromachi period. * Mikoshi Ridgepole Plaques: Five plaques dating from 1547 (Tenbun 16) to 1852 (Kaei 5). * Pair of Lions: From the Kamakura period. == Information == ; {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yoshida Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yoshida shrine.jpg|270px|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi) worship hall]]
Worship Hall |address = [[2 Sekiya-cho, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture|愛知県豊橋市関屋町2番地]] |coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-23|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] |Rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] |established = (Tradition) [[:en:702 AD|702 AD]] (Taihō 2) |main_hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] |alias = |annual_festival = [[July]] (third [[Friday]], [[Saturday]], and [[Sunday]]) |rituals = Yoritomo Procession |map = {{location map|Japan Aichi Prefecture|label=Yoshida Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Yoshida Shrine''' (吉田神社) is a Shinto shrine located at {{ill|Sekiya-cho|ja|関屋町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekiya-cho|qq=}}, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture. Under the [[Modern typeing System|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was designated a Prefectural Shrine in [[:en:1921|1921]], having been elevated from a {{ill|village shrine|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Deities == The primary deity enshrined is: * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (素戔嗚尊){{Cite book|title=Rediscovering Our Hometown Guidebook: Learning More About Toyohashi, Vol. 4|date=March 19, 2009|publisher=Toyohashi City Public Relations Division|page=3}} Additionally, [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] is enshrined as a complementary deity. == History == [[File:Yoshida, Tenno Festival on the Fifteenth Day of the Sixth Month LACMA M.66.35.23.jpg|thumb|Yoshida Shrine Tennō Festival]] The shrine is said to originate from the temporary palace established during Empress Jitō's visit to Mikawa Province in 702 (Taihō 2). Following her passing, the palace was converted into a shrine dedicated to her. Early records, such as the ''Wakan Sansai Zue,'' refer to it as a "Tennō Shrine enshrining Empress Jitō." During the Edo period, shrine legends record ceremonies honoring her. In 1124 (Tenji 1), during an epidemic, the {{ill|Yokochi clan|ja|横地氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokochi clan|qq=}}, shrine priests, invited [[Gozu Tennō]], the guardian deity of [[:en:Jetavana|Jetavana monastery]], from the {{ill|Kanshin-in|ja|感神院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanshin-in|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園信仰}} (present-day [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]]) to suppress the disease. Subsequently, the shrine was called "Gozu Tennō Shrine" or "Yoshida Tennō Shrine." In 1178 (Jishō 2), during his exile in Izu, [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] sent an envoy, Suzuki Jūrō Mototoshi, to pray at Yoshida Shrine. In 1186 (Bunji 2), he dispatched Ishida Jirō Tamehisa for the same purpose. During the Kenkyū era, he ordered Adachi Tōkurō Morinaga, the protector of Mikawa, to rebuild the shrine. In 1507 (Eishō 3), following the construction of [[:en:Yoshida Castle (Mikawa Province)|Yoshida Castle]], {{ill|Makino Kokuhaku|ja|牧野古白|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makino Kokuhaku|qq=}} rebuilt the shrine as the castle’s guardian shrine. In 1548 (Tenbun 16), [[:en:Imagawa Yoshimoto|Imagawa Yoshimoto]] donated a mikoshi, initiating the shrine’s mikoshi procession. The inscription on the mikoshi’s ridgepole plaque, written by [[:en:Sessai Chōrō|Taigen Sessai]], reads: “Peace throughout the land, safety of the nation, harmony among the people.” During the Edo period, the shrine received patronage from successive [[:en:Yoshida Domain|lords of Yoshida Domain]], resulting in several reconstructions. In 1837 (Tenpō 6), it received the highest Rank of ''Shoichii'' (Senior First Rank). The shrine’s festivals, influenced by [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]] in Kyoto, were well-known and recorded in various festival Rankings. Following the abolition of domains during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the shrine’s name changed to Yoshida Shrine under the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}}, and it was reorganized with Susanoo-no-Mikoto as its deity. == Toyohashi Gion Festival == {{main|{{ill|Toyohashi Gion Festival|ja|豊橋祇園祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohashi Gion Festival|qq=}}}} == Precincts == File:吉田神社（豊橋）鳥居.JPG|Entrance Stone Torii File:吉田神社（豊橋）祭壇.JPG|Inner Sanctuary File:吉田神社（豊橋）摂社.JPG|Kin Kanmaru and Castle Guardian Inari Shrine File:吉田神社（豊橋市） - 手筒花火発祥之地 石碑.jpg|Handheld Fireworks Origin Monument File:吉田神社（豊橋市） - 手筒花火1.jpg|Large Fireworks File:吉田神社（豊橋）境内より.JPG|View from the Precinct File:Toyohashi Gion Maturi.JPG|Handheld Fireworks Dedication at Toyohashi Gion Festival == Keidai-sha (境内社) == * Inari-sha (稲荷社) ** Saijin: {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|宇迦之魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} * Izawa-sha (伊雑社) ** Saijin: {{ill|Izawa-no-miya|lt=Isahotomi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊佐波止美命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izawa-no-miya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊雑宮}} * Miketsu-sha (御食社) ** Saijin: {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|宇迦之魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, {{ill|Izawa-no-miya|lt=Tamabashiraya-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|玉柱屋比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izawa-no-miya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊雑宮}} == Cultural Properties == [[File:吉田天王社祭礼図.jpg|thumb|580x580px|Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting]] '''Toyohashi City Designated Folk Cultural Properties''' * Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting (including donor inscriptions){{Cite web|date=May 7, 2021|url=http://www.toyohashi-bihaku.jp/?p=14012|title=Yoshida Shrine Items Designated as City Cultural Properties|publisher=Toyohashi City Museum of Art and History|accessdate=April 19, 2022}} : Dimensions: approximately 1.5 m by 3.6 m{{Cite web|date=May 8, 2021|url=http://www.higashiaichi.co.jp/news/detail/7922|title="Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting" Designated as a Folk Cultural Property|publisher=Higashi Aichi Newspaper|accessdate=April 19, 2022}}. Painted by Hatake Achi in 1890 (Meiji 23). The painting depicts festival scenes from the late Edo to early Meiji periods. '''Toyohashi City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties''' * Lion Heads: Created during the Nanboku-chō to Muromachi periods. * Demon Faces: From the Muromachi period. * Mikoshi Ridgepole Plaques: Five plaques dating from 1547 (Tenbun 16) to 1852 (Kaei 5). * Pair of Lions: From the Kamakura period. == Information == ; Address * {{ill|2 Sekiya-cho, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県豊橋市関屋町2番地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2 Sekiya-cho, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; Access * Nearest station: [[:en:Toyohashi Railroad|Toyohashi Railroad]], {{ill|Shiyakusho-mae Station (Aichi)|ja|市役所前停留場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiyakusho-mae Station (Aichi)|qq=}} (approx. 5-minute walk) * Nearest station: [[:en:Meitetsu|Meitetsu]], [[:en:Toyohashi Station|Toyohashi Station]] (approx. 20-minute walk) == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Toyohashi Gion Festival|ja|豊橋祇園祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohashi Gion Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:Tezutsu-hanabi|Tezutsu-hanabi]] * {{ill|Sugimori Hachiman Shrine|ja|杉森八幡社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugimori Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Watanabe Koka|ja|渡辺小崋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watanabe Koka|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoshida-jinja (Toyohashi)}} * [http://toyohashi-yoshida.com Yoshida Shrine Official Website] * [http://toyohashigion.org/index.html Toyohashi Gion Festival Support Association] * [http://www.honokuni.or.jp/toyohashi/spot/000062.html Toyohashi Tourism Convention Association] * [https://www.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/wo06/wo06_3145_56/] Festival Guidebook * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DwAR4KaisJI Yoshida Shrine Toyohashi Gion Festival Handheld Fireworks] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|吉田神社 (豊橋市)|version=101623112|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)]] [[ja:吉田神社 (豊橋市)]] [[ja:吉田神社]] [[Category:Yasaka-jinja]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:愛知県内のMunicipal designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Toyohashi]] [[Category:History of Toyohashi, Aichi]] {{Commons category|Yoshida-jinja (Toyohashi)}}

The inscription on the mikoshi’s ridgepole plaque, written by [[:en:Sessai Chōrō|Taigen Sessai]], reads: “Peace throughout the land, safety of the nation, harmony among the people.” During the Edo period, the shrine received patronage from successive [[:en:Yoshida Domain|lords of Yoshida Domain]], resulting in several reconstructions. In 1837 (Tenpō 6), it received the highest Rank of ''Shoichii'' (Senior First Rank). The shrine’s festivals, influenced by [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]] in Kyoto, were well-known and recorded in various festival Rankings. Following the abolition of domains during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the shrine’s name changed to Yoshida Shrine under the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}}, and it was reorganized with Susanoo-no-Mikoto as its deity. == Toyohashi Gion Festival == {{main|{{ill|Toyohashi Gion Festival|ja|豊橋祇園祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohashi Gion Festival|qq=}}}} == Precincts == File:吉田神社（豊橋）鳥居.JPG|Entrance Stone Torii File:吉田神社（豊橋）祭壇.JPG|Inner Sanctuary File:吉田神社（豊橋）摂社.JPG|Kin Kanmaru and Castle Guardian Inari Shrine File:吉田神社（豊橋市） - 手筒花火発祥之地 石碑.jpg|Handheld Fireworks Origin Monument File:吉田神社（豊橋市） - 手筒花火1.jpg|Large Fireworks File:吉田神社（豊橋）境内より.JPG|View from the Precinct File:Toyohashi Gion Maturi.JPG|Handheld Fireworks Dedication at Toyohashi Gion Festival == Keidai-sha (境内社) == * Inari-sha (稲荷社) ** Saijin: {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|宇迦之魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} * Izawa-sha (伊雑社) ** Saijin: {{ill|Izawa-no-miya|lt=Isahotomi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊佐波止美命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izawa-no-miya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊雑宮}} * Miketsu-sha (御食社) ** Saijin: {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|宇迦之魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, {{ill|Izawa-no-miya|lt=Tamabashiraya-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|玉柱屋比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izawa-no-miya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊雑宮}} == Cultural Properties == [[File:吉田天王社祭礼図.jpg|thumb|580x580px|Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting]] '''Toyohashi City Designated Folk Cultural Properties''' * Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting (including donor inscriptions){{Cite web|date=May 7, 2021|url=http://www.toyohashi-bihaku.jp/?p=14012|title=Yoshida Shrine Items Designated as City Cultural Properties|publisher=Toyohashi City Museum of Art and History|accessdate=April 19, 2022}} : Dimensions: approximately 1.5 m by 3.6 m{{Cite web|date=May 8, 2021|url=http://www.higashiaichi.co.jp/news/detail/7922|title="Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting" Designated as a Folk Cultural Property|publisher=Higashi Aichi Newspaper|accessdate=April 19, 2022}}. Painted by Hatake Achi in 1890 (Meiji 23). The painting depicts festival scenes from the late Edo to early Meiji periods. '''Toyohashi City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties''' * Lion Heads: Created during the Nanboku-chō to Muromachi periods. * Demon Faces: From the Muromachi period. * Mikoshi Ridgepole Plaques: Five plaques dating from 1547 (Tenbun 16) to 1852 (Kaei 5). * Pair of Lions: From the Kamakura period. == Information == ; Address * {{ill|2 Sekiya-cho, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県豊橋市関屋町2番地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2 Sekiya-cho, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; Access * Nearest station: [[:en:Toyohashi Railroad|Toyohashi Railroad]], {{ill|Shiyakusho-mae Station (Aichi)|ja|市役所前停留場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiyakusho-mae Station (Aichi)|qq=}} (approx. 5-minute walk) * Nearest station: [[:en:Meitetsu|Meitetsu]], [[:en:Toyohashi Station|Toyohashi Station]] (approx. 20-minute walk) == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Toyohashi Gion Festival|ja|豊橋祇園祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohashi Gion Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:Tezutsu-hanabi|Tezutsu-hanabi]] * {{ill|Sugimori Hachiman Shrine|ja|杉森八幡社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugimori Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Watanabe Koka|ja|渡辺小崋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watanabe Koka|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoshida-jinja (Toyohashi)}} * [http://toyohashi-yoshida.com Yoshida Shrine Official Website] * [http://toyohashigion.org/index.html Toyohashi Gion Festival Support Association] * [http://www.honokuni.or.jp/toyohashi/spot/000062.html Toyohashi Tourism Convention Association] * [https://www.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/wo06/wo06_3145_56/] Festival Guidebook * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DwAR4KaisJI Yoshida Shrine Toyohashi Gion Festival Handheld Fireworks] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|吉田神社 (豊橋市)|version=101623112|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)]] [[ja:吉田神社 (豊橋市)]] [[ja:吉田神社]] [[Category:Yasaka-jinja]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Toyohashi]] [[Category:History of Toyohashi, Aichi]] {{Commons category|Yoshida-jinja (Toyohashi)}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Yoshida-jinja (Toyohashi)]][[ja:吉田神社 (豊橋市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q24898358]] Wikidata name: Yoshida-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yoshida Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:吉田神社（豊橋）拝殿.JPG |address = [[2 Sekiya-cho, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture|愛知県豊橋市関屋町2番地]] |coordinates = {{Coord|34.77027777777778|N|137.38991666666666|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] |Rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] |established = (Tradition) [[:en:702 AD|702 AD]] (Taihō 2) |main_hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] |alias = |annual_festival = [[July]] (third [[Friday]], [[Saturday]], and [[Sunday]]) |rituals = Yoritomo Procession |map = {{location map|Japan Aichi Prefecture|label=Yoshida Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} |location = {{ill|Toyohashi|qid=Q336431}} }} '''Yoshida Shrine''' (吉田神社) is a Shinto shrine located at {{ill|Sekiya-cho|ja|関屋町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekiya-cho|qq=|1=Sekiya-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109596689}}, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture. Under the [[Modern typeing System|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was designated a Prefectural Shrine in [[:en:1921|1921]], having been elevated from a {{ill|village shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Deities == The primary deity enshrined is: * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (素戔嗚尊)<ref>{{Cite book|title=Rediscovering Our Hometown Guidebook: Learning More About Toyohashi, Vol. 4|date=March 19, 2009|publisher=Toyohashi City Public Relations Division|page=3}}</ref> Additionally, [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] is enshrined as a complementary deity.
== History == [[File:Yoshida, Tenno Festival on the Fifteenth Day of the Sixth Month LACMA M.66.35.23.jpg|thumb|Yoshida Shrine Tennō Festival]] The shrine is said to originate from the temporary palace established during Empress Jitō's visit to Mikawa Province in 702 (Taihō 2). Following her passing, the palace was converted into a shrine dedicated to her. Early records, such as the ''Wakan Sansai Zue,'' refer to it as a "Tennō Shrine enshrining Empress Jitō." During the Edo period, shrine legends record ceremonies honoring her.
In 1124 (Tenji 1), during an epidemic, the {{ill|Yokochi clan|ja|横地氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokochi clan|qq=}}, shrine priests, invited [[Gozu Tennō]], the guardian deity of [[:en:Jetavana|Jetavana monastery]], from the {{ill|Kanshin-in|ja|感神院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanshin-in|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園信仰|1=Gion Faith|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588425}} (present-day [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]]) to suppress the disease. Subsequently, the shrine was called "Gozu Tennō Shrine" or "Yoshida Tennō Shrine."
In 1178 (Jishō 2), during his exile in Izu, [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] sent an envoy, Suzuki Jūrō Mototoshi, to pray at Yoshida Shrine. In 1186 (Bunji 2), he dispatched Ishida Jirō Tamehisa for the same purpose. During the Kenkyū era, he ordered Adachi Tōkurō Morinaga, the protector of Mikawa, to rebuild the shrine.
In 1507 (Eishō 3), following the construction of [[:en:Yoshida Castle (Mikawa Province)|Yoshida Castle]], {{ill|Makino Kokuhaku|ja|牧野古白|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makino Kokuhaku|qq=|1=Makino Kohaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10860041}} rebuilt the shrine as the castle’s guardian shrine. In 1548 (Tenbun 16), [[:en:Imagawa Yoshimoto|Imagawa Yoshimoto]] donated a mikoshi, initiating the shrine’s mikoshi procession. The inscription on the mikoshi’s ridgepole plaque, written by [[:en:Sessai Chōrō|Taigen Sessai]], reads: “Peace throughout the land, safety of the nation, harmony among the people.”
During the Edo period, the shrine received patronage from successive [[:en:Yoshida Domain|lords of Yoshida Domain]], resulting in several reconstructions. In 1837 (Tenpō 6), it received the highest Rank of ''Shoichii'' (Senior First Rank). The shrine’s festivals, influenced by [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]] in Kyoto, were well-known and recorded in various festival Rankings.
Following the abolition of domains during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the shrine’s name changed to Yoshida Shrine under the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}}, and it was reorganized with Susanoo-no-Mikoto as its deity.
== Toyohashi Gion Festival == {{main|{{ill|Toyohashi Gion Festival|ja|豊橋祇園祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohashi Gion Festival|qq=}}}}
== Precincts == <gallery> File:吉田神社（豊橋）鳥居.JPG|Entrance Stone Torii File:吉田神社（豊橋）祭壇.JPG|Inner Sanctuary File:吉田神社（豊橋）摂社.JPG|Kin Kanmaru and Castle Guardian Inari Shrine File:吉田神社（豊橋市） - 手筒花火発祥之地 石碑.jpg|Handheld Fireworks Origin Monument File:吉田神社（豊橋市） - 手筒花火1.jpg|Large Fireworks File:吉田神社（豊橋）境内より.JPG|View from the Precinct File:Toyohashi Gion Maturi.JPG|Handheld Fireworks Dedication at Toyohashi Gion Festival </gallery>
== Keidai-sha (境内社) == * Inari-sha (稲荷社) ** Saijin: {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|宇迦之魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} * Izawa-sha (伊雑社) ** Saijin: {{ill|Izawa-no-miya|lt=Isahotomi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊佐波止美命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izawa-no-miya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊雑宮}} * Miketsu-sha (御食社) ** Saijin: {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|宇迦之魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, {{ill|Izawa-no-miya|lt=Tamabashiraya-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|玉柱屋比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izawa-no-miya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊雑宮}}
== Cultural Properties == [[File:吉田天王社祭礼図.jpg|thumb|580x580px|Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting]] '''Toyohashi City Designated Folk Cultural Properties''' * Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting (including donor inscriptions)<ref name=bunkazaicenter>{{Cite web|date=May 7, 2021|url=http://www.toyohashi-bihaku.jp/?p=14012|title=Yoshida Shrine Items Designated as City Cultural Properties|publisher=Toyohashi City Museum of Art and History|accessdate=April 19, 2022}}</ref> : Dimensions: approximately 1.5 m by 3.6 m<ref name=higashiaichi1>{{Cite web|date=May 8, 2021|url=http://www.higashiaichi.co.jp/news/detail/7922|title="Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting" Designated as a Folk Cultural Property|publisher=Higashi Aichi Newspaper|accessdate=April 19, 2022}}</ref>. Painted by Hatake Achi in 1890 (Meiji 23). The painting depicts festival scenes from the late Edo to early Meiji periods.
'''Toyohashi City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties''' * Lion Heads: Created during the Nanboku-chō to Muromachi periods<ref name=bunkazaicenter />. * Demon Faces: From the Muromachi period<ref name=bunkazaicenter />. * Mikoshi Ridgepole Plaques: Five plaques dating from 1547 (Tenbun 16) to 1852 (Kaei 5)<ref name=yoshidajinja />. * Pair of Lions: From the Kamakura period<ref name=yoshidajinja />.
== Information == ; Address * {{ill|2 Sekiya-cho, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県豊橋市関屋町2番地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2 Sekiya-cho, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; Access * Nearest station: [[:en:Toyohashi Railroad|Toyohashi Railroad]], {{ill|Shiyakusho-mae Station (Aichi)|ja|市役所前停留場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiyakusho-mae Station (Aichi)|qq=|1=Shiyakusho-mae Station (Aichi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16281111}} (approx. 5-minute walk) * Nearest station: [[:en:Meitetsu|Meitetsu]], [[:en:Toyohashi Station|Toyohashi Station]] (approx. 20-minute walk)
== Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Toyohashi Gion Festival|ja|豊橋祇園祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohashi Gion Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:Tezutsu-hanabi|Tezutsu-hanabi]] * {{ill|Sugimori Hachiman Shrine|ja|杉森八幡社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugimori Hachiman Shrine|qq=|1=Sugimori Hachimansha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522173}} * {{ill|Watanabe Koka|ja|渡辺小崋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watanabe Koka|qq=|1=Shōka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11562224}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoshida-jinja (Toyohashi)}} * [http://toyohashi-yoshida.com Yoshida Shrine Official Website] * [http://toyohashigion.org/index.html Toyohashi Gion Festival Support Association] * [http://www.honokuni.or.jp/toyohashi/spot/000062.html Toyohashi Tourism Convention Association] * [https://www.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/wo06/wo06_3145_56/] Festival Guidebook * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DwAR4KaisJI Yoshida Shrine Toyohashi Gion Festival Handheld Fireworks]

In 1507 (Eishō 3), following the construction of [[:en:Yoshida Castle (Mikawa Province)|Yoshida Castle]], {{ill|Makino Kokuhaku|ja|牧野古白|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makino Kokuhaku|qq=|1=Makino Kohaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10860041}} rebuilt the shrine as the castle’s guardian shrine. In 1548 (Tenbun 16), [[:en:Imagawa Yoshimoto|Imagawa Yoshimoto]] donated a mikoshi, initiating the shrine’s mikoshi procession. The inscription on the mikoshi’s ridgepole plaque, written by [[:en:Sessai Chōrō|Taigen Sessai]], reads: “Peace throughout the land, safety of the nation, harmony among the people.”
During the Edo period, the shrine received patronage from successive [[:en:Yoshida Domain|lords of Yoshida Domain]], resulting in several reconstructions. Following the abolition of domains during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the shrine’s name changed to Yoshida Shrine under the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}}, and it was reorganized with Susanoo-no-Mikoto as its deity.
== Toyohashi Gion Festival == {{main|{{ill|Toyohashi Gion Festival|ja|豊橋祇園祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohashi Gion Festival|qq=}}}}

== Cultural Properties == [[File:吉田天王社祭礼図.jpg|thumb|580x580px|Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting]] '''Toyohashi City Designated Folk Cultural Properties''' * Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting (including donor inscriptions)<ref name=bunkazaicenter>{{Cite web|date=May 7, 2021|url=http://www.toyohashi-bihaku.jp/?p=14012|title=Yoshida Shrine Items Designated as City Cultural Properties|publisher=Toyohashi City Museum of Art and History|accessdate=April 19, 2022}}</ref> : Dimensions: approximately 1.5 m by 3.6 m<ref name=higashiaichi1>{{Cite web|date=May 8, 2021|url=http://www.higashiaichi.co.jp/news/detail/7922|title="Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting" Designated as a Folk Cultural Property|publisher=Higashi Aichi Newspaper|accessdate=April 19, 2022}}</ref>. Painted by Hatake Achi in 1890 (Meiji 23). The painting depicts festival scenes from the late Edo to early Meiji periods.
'''Toyohashi City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties''' * Lion Heads: Created during the Nanboku-chō to Muromachi periods<ref name=bunkazaicenter />. * Demon Faces: From the Muromachi period<ref name=bunkazaicenter />. * Mikoshi Ridgepole Plaques: Five plaques dating from 1547 (Tenbun 16) to 1852 (Kaei 5)<ref name=yoshidajinja />. * Pair of Lions: From the Kamakura period<ref name=yoshidajinja />.

[[Category:Buildings and structures in Toyohashi]] [[Category:History of Toyohashi, Aichi]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Yoshida-jinja (Toyohashi)}} {{wikidata link|Q24898358}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yoshida-jinja (Toyohashi)]][[ja:吉田神社 (豊橋市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q24898358]] Wikidata name: Yoshida-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yoshida Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:吉田神社（豊橋）拝殿.JPG |address = [[2 Sekiya-cho, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture|愛知県豊橋市関屋町2番地]] |coordinates = {{Coord|34.77027777777778|N|137.38991666666666|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] |Rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] |established = (Tradition) [[:en:702 AD|702 AD]] (Taihō 2) |main_hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] |alias = |annual_festival = [[July]] (third [[Friday]], [[Saturday]], and [[Sunday]]) |rituals = Yoritomo Procession |map = {{location map|Japan Aichi Prefecture|label=Yoshida Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} |location = {{ill|Toyohashi|WD=Q336431}} }} '''Yoshida Shrine''' (吉田神社) is a Shinto shrine located at {{ill|Sekiya-cho|ja|関屋町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekiya-cho|qq=|1=Sekiya-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109596689}}, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture. Under the [[Modern typeing System|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was designated a Prefectural Shrine in [[:en:1921|1921]], having been elevated from a {{ill|village shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Deities == The primary deity enshrined is: * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (素戔嗚尊)<ref>{{Cite book|title=Rediscovering Our Hometown Guidebook: Learning More About Toyohashi, Vol. 4|date=March 19, 2009|publisher=Toyohashi City Public Relations Division|page=3}}</ref> Additionally, [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] is enshrined as a complementary deity.
== History == [[File:Yoshida, Tenno Festival on the Fifteenth Day of the Sixth Month LACMA M.66.35.23.jpg|thumb|Yoshida Shrine Tennō Festival]] The shrine is said to originate from the temporary palace established during Empress Jitō's visit to Mikawa Province in 702 (Taihō 2). Following her passing, the palace was converted into a shrine dedicated to her. Early records, such as the ''Wakan Sansai Zue,'' refer to it as a "Tennō Shrine enshrining Empress Jitō." During the Edo period, shrine legends record ceremonies honoring her.
In 1124 (Tenji 1), during an epidemic, the {{ill|Yokochi clan|ja|横地氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokochi clan|qq=}}, shrine priests, invited [[Gozu Tennō]], the guardian deity of [[:en:Jetavana|Jetavana monastery]], from the {{ill|Kanshin-in|ja|感神院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanshin-in|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園信仰|1=Gion Faith|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588425}} (present-day [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]]) to suppress the disease. Subsequently, the shrine was called "Gozu Tennō Shrine" or "Yoshida Tennō Shrine."
In 1178 (Jishō 2), during his exile in Izu, [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] sent an envoy, Suzuki Jūrō Mototoshi, to pray at Yoshida Shrine. In 1186 (Bunji 2), he dispatched Ishida Jirō Tamehisa for the same purpose. During the Kenkyū era, he ordered Adachi Tōkurō Morinaga, the protector of Mikawa, to rebuild the shrine.
In 1507 (Eishō 3), following the construction of [[:en:Yoshida Castle (Mikawa Province)|Yoshida Castle]], {{ill|Makino Kokuhaku|ja|牧野古白|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makino Kokuhaku|qq=|1=Makino Kohaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10860041}} rebuilt the shrine as the castle’s guardian shrine. In 1548 (Tenbun 16), [[:en:Imagawa Yoshimoto|Imagawa Yoshimoto]] donated a mikoshi, initiating the shrine’s mikoshi procession. The inscription on the mikoshi’s ridgepole plaque, written by [[:en:Sessai Chōrō|Taigen Sessai]], reads: “Peace throughout the land, safety of the nation, harmony among the people.”
During the Edo period, the shrine received patronage from successive [[:en:Yoshida Domain|lords of Yoshida Domain]], resulting in several reconstructions. In 1837 (Tenpō 6), it received the highest Rank of ''Shoichii'' (Senior First Rank). The shrine’s festivals, influenced by [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]] in Kyoto, were well-known and recorded in various festival Rankings.
Following the abolition of domains during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the shrine’s name changed to Yoshida Shrine under the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}}, and it was reorganized with Susanoo-no-Mikoto as its deity.

== Cultural Properties == [[File:吉田天王社祭礼図.jpg|thumb|580x580px|Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting]] '''Toyohashi City Designated Folk Cultural Properties''' * Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting (including donor inscriptions)<ref name=bunkazaicenter>{{Cite web|date=May 7, 2021|url=http://www.toyohashi-bihaku.jp/?p=14012|title=Yoshida Shrine Items Designated as City Cultural Properties|publisher=Toyohashi City Museum of Art and History|accessdate=April 19, 2022}}</ref> : Dimensions: approximately 1.5 m by 3.6 m<ref name=higashiaichi1>{{Cite web|date=May 8, 2021|url=http://www.higashiaichi.co.jp/news/detail/7922|title="Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting" Designated as a Folk Cultural Property|publisher=Higashi Aichi Newspaper|accessdate=April 19, 2022}}</ref>. Painted by Hatake Achi in 1890 (Meiji 23). The painting depicts festival scenes from the late Edo to early Meiji periods.
'''Toyohashi City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties''' * Lion Heads: Created during the Nanboku-chō to Muromachi periods<ref name=bunkazaicenter />. * Demon Faces: From the Muromachi period<ref name=bunkazaicenter />. * Mikoshi Ridgepole Plaques: Five plaques dating from 1547 (Tenbun 16) to 1852 (Kaei 5)<ref name=yoshidajinja />. * Pair of Lions: From the Kamakura period<ref name=yoshidajinja />.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yoshida-jinja (Toyohashi)]][[ja:吉田神社 (豊橋市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q24898358]] Wikidata name: Yoshida-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yoshida Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:吉田神社（豊橋）拝殿.JPG |address = [[2 Sekiya-cho, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture|愛知県豊橋市関屋町2番地]] |coordinates = {{Coord|34.77027777777778|N|137.38991666666666|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] |Rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] |established = (Tradition) [[:en:702 AD|702 AD]] (Taihō 2) |main_hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] |alias = |annual_festival = [[July]] (third [[Friday]], [[Saturday]], and [[Sunday]]) |rituals = Yoritomo Procession |map = {{location map|Japan Aichi Prefecture|label=Yoshida Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} |location = {{ill|Toyohashi|WD=Q336431}} }} '''Yoshida Shrine''' (吉田神社) is a Shinto shrine located at {{ill|Sekiya-cho|ja|関屋町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekiya-cho|1=Sekiya-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109596689}}, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture. Under the [[Modern typeing System|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was designated a Prefectural Shrine in [[:en:1921|1921]], having been elevated from a {{ill|village shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Deities == The primary deity enshrined is: * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (素戔嗚尊)<ref>{{Cite book|title=Rediscovering Our Hometown Guidebook: Learning More About Toyohashi, Vol. 4|date=March 19, 2009|publisher=Toyohashi City Public Relations Division|page=3}}</ref> Additionally, [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] is enshrined as a complementary deity.
== History == [[File:Yoshida, Tenno Festival on the Fifteenth Day of the Sixth Month LACMA M.66.35.23.jpg|thumb|Yoshida Shrine Tennō Festival]] The shrine is said to originate from the temporary palace established during Empress Jitō's visit to Mikawa Province in 702 (Taihō 2). Following her passing, the palace was converted into a shrine dedicated to her. Early records, such as the ''Wakan Sansai Zue,'' refer to it as a "Tennō Shrine enshrining Empress Jitō." During the Edo period, shrine legends record ceremonies honoring her.
In 1124 (Tenji 1), during an epidemic, the {{ill|Yokochi clan|ja|横地氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokochi clan|qq=}}, shrine priests, invited [[Gozu Tennō]], the guardian deity of [[:en:Jetavana|Jetavana monastery]], from the {{ill|Kanshin-in|ja|感神院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanshin-in|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園信仰|1=Gion Faith|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588425}} (present-day [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]]) to suppress the disease. Subsequently, the shrine was called "Gozu Tennō Shrine" or "Yoshida Tennō Shrine."
In 1178 (Jishō 2), during his exile in Izu, [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] sent an envoy, Suzuki Jūrō Mototoshi, to pray at Yoshida Shrine. In 1186 (Bunji 2), he dispatched Ishida Jirō Tamehisa for the same purpose. During the Kenkyū era, he ordered Adachi Tōkurō Morinaga, the protector of Mikawa, to rebuild the shrine.
In 1507 (Eishō 3), following the construction of [[:en:Yoshida Castle (Mikawa Province)|Yoshida Castle]], {{ill|Makino Kokuhaku|ja|牧野古白|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makino Kokuhaku|1=Makino Kohaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10860041}} rebuilt the shrine as the castle’s guardian shrine. In 1548 (Tenbun 16), [[:en:Imagawa Yoshimoto|Imagawa Yoshimoto]] donated a mikoshi, initiating the shrine’s mikoshi procession. The inscription on the mikoshi’s ridgepole plaque, written by [[:en:Sessai Chōrō|Taigen Sessai]], reads: “Peace throughout the land, safety of the nation, harmony among the people.”
During the Edo period, the shrine received patronage from successive [[:en:Yoshida Domain|lords of Yoshida Domain]], resulting in several reconstructions. In 1837 (Tenpō 6), it received the highest Rank of ''Shoichii'' (Senior First Rank). The shrine’s festivals, influenced by [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]] in Kyoto, were well-known and recorded in various festival Rankings.
Following the abolition of domains during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the shrine’s name changed to Yoshida Shrine under the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}}, and it was reorganized with Susanoo-no-Mikoto as its deity.

== Keidai-sha (境内社) == * Inari-sha (稲荷社) ** Saijin: {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|宇迦之魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} * Izawa-sha (伊雑社) ** Saijin: {{ill|Izawa-no-miya|lt=Isahotomi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊佐波止美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izawa-no-miya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊雑宮}} * Miketsu-sha (御食社) ** Saijin: {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|宇迦之魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, {{ill|Izawa-no-miya|lt=Tamabashiraya-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|玉柱屋比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izawa-no-miya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊雑宮}}
== Cultural Properties == [[File:吉田天王社祭礼図.jpg|thumb|580x580px|Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting]] '''Toyohashi City Designated Folk Cultural Properties''' * Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting (including donor inscriptions)<ref name=bunkazaicenter>{{Cite web|date=May 7, 2021|url=http://www.toyohashi-bihaku.jp/?p=14012|title=Yoshida Shrine Items Designated as City Cultural Properties|publisher=Toyohashi City Museum of Art and History|accessdate=April 19, 2022}}</ref> : Dimensions: approximately 1.5 m by 3.6 m<ref name=higashiaichi1>{{Cite web|date=May 8, 2021|url=http://www.higashiaichi.co.jp/news/detail/7922|title="Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting" Designated as a Folk Cultural Property|publisher=Higashi Aichi Newspaper|accessdate=April 19, 2022}}</ref>. Painted by Hatake Achi in 1890 (Meiji 23). The painting depicts festival scenes from the late Edo to early Meiji periods.
'''Toyohashi City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties''' * Lion Heads: Created during the Nanboku-chō to Muromachi periods<ref name=bunkazaicenter />. * Demon Faces: From the Muromachi period<ref name=bunkazaicenter />. * Mikoshi Ridgepole Plaques: Five plaques dating from 1547 (Tenbun 16) to 1852 (Kaei 5)<ref name=yoshidajinja />. * Pair of Lions: From the Kamakura period<ref name=yoshidajinja />.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yoshida-jinja (Toyohashi)]][[ja:吉田神社 (豊橋市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q24898358]] Wikidata name: Yoshida-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yoshida Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:吉田神社（豊橋）拝殿.JPG |address = [[2 Sekiya-cho, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture|愛知県豊橋市関屋町2番地]] |coordinates = {{Coord|34.77027777777778|N|137.38991666666666|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] |Rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] |established = (Tradition) [[:en:702 AD|702 AD]] (Taihō 2) |main_hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] |alias = |annual_festival = [[July]] (third [[Friday]], [[Saturday]], and [[Sunday]]) |rituals = Yoritomo Procession |map = {{location map|Japan Aichi Prefecture|label=Yoshida Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} |location = {{ill|Toyohashi|WD=Q336431}} }} '''Yoshida Shrine''' (吉田神社) is a Shinto shrine located at {{ill|Sekiya-cho|ja|関屋町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekiya-cho|1=Sekiya-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109596689}}, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture. Under the [[Modern typeing System|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was designated a Prefectural Shrine in [[:en:1921|1921]], having been elevated from a {{ill|village shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Deities == The primary deity enshrined is: * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (素戔嗚尊)<ref>{{Cite book|title=Rediscovering Our Hometown Guidebook: Learning More About Toyohashi, Vol. 4|date=March 19, 2009|publisher=Toyohashi City Public Relations Division|page=3}}</ref> Additionally, [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] is enshrined as a complementary deity.
== History == [[File:Yoshida, Tenno Festival on the Fifteenth Day of the Sixth Month LACMA M.66.35.23.jpg|thumb|Yoshida Shrine Tennō Festival]] The shrine is said to originate from the temporary palace established during Empress Jitō's visit to Mikawa Province in 702 (Taihō 2). Following her passing, the palace was converted into a shrine dedicated to her. Early records, such as the ''Wakan Sansai Zue,'' refer to it as a "Tennō Shrine enshrining Empress Jitō." During the Edo period, shrine legends record ceremonies honoring her.
In 1124 (Tenji 1), during an epidemic, the {{ill|Yokochi clan|ja|横地氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokochi clan|qq=}}, shrine priests, invited [[Gozu Tennō]], the guardian deity of [[:en:Jetavana|Jetavana monastery]], from the {{ill|Kanshin-in|ja|感神院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanshin-in|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園信仰|1=Gion Faith|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588425}} (present-day [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]]) to suppress the disease. Subsequently, the shrine was called "Gozu Tennō Shrine" or "Yoshida Tennō Shrine."
In 1178 (Jishō 2), during his exile in Izu, [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] sent an envoy, Suzuki Jūrō Mototoshi, to pray at Yoshida Shrine. In 1186 (Bunji 2), he dispatched Ishida Jirō Tamehisa for the same purpose. During the Kenkyū era, he ordered Adachi Tōkurō Morinaga, the protector of Mikawa, to rebuild the shrine.
In 1507 (Eishō 3), following the construction of [[:en:Yoshida Castle (Mikawa Province)|Yoshida Castle]], {{ill|Makino Kokuhaku|ja|牧野古白|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makino Kokuhaku|1=Makino Kohaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10860041}} rebuilt the shrine as the castle’s guardian shrine. In 1548 (Tenbun 16), [[:en:Imagawa Yoshimoto|Imagawa Yoshimoto]] donated a mikoshi, initiating the shrine’s mikoshi procession. The inscription on the mikoshi’s ridgepole plaque, written by [[:en:Sessai Chōrō|Taigen Sessai]], reads: “Peace throughout the land, safety of the nation, harmony among the people.”
During the Edo period, the shrine received patronage from successive [[:en:Yoshida Domain|lords of Yoshida Domain]], resulting in several reconstructions. In 1837 (Tenpō 6), it received the highest Rank of ''Shoichii'' (Senior First Rank). The shrine’s festivals, influenced by [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]] in Kyoto, were well-known and recorded in various festival Rankings.
Following the abolition of domains during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the shrine’s name changed to Yoshida Shrine under the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}}, and it was reorganized with Susanoo-no-Mikoto as its deity.
== Toyohashi Gion Festival == {{main|{{ill|Toyohashi Gion Festival|ja|豊橋祇園祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohashi Gion Festival|qq=}}}}

== Cultural Properties == [[File:吉田天王社祭礼図.jpg|thumb|580x580px|Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting]] '''Toyohashi City Designated Folk Cultural Properties''' * Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting (including donor inscriptions)<ref name=bunkazaicenter>{{Cite web|date=May 7, 2021|url=http://www.toyohashi-bihaku.jp/?p=14012|title=Yoshida Shrine Items Designated as City Cultural Properties|publisher=Toyohashi City Museum of Art and History|accessdate=April 19, 2022}}</ref> : Dimensions: approximately 1.5 m by 3.6 m<ref name=higashiaichi1>{{Cite web|date=May 8, 2021|url=http://www.higashiaichi.co.jp/news/detail/7922|title="Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting" Designated as a Folk Cultural Property|publisher=Higashi Aichi Newspaper|accessdate=April 19, 2022}}</ref>. Painted by Hatake Achi in 1890 (Meiji 23). The painting depicts festival scenes from the late Edo to early Meiji periods.
'''Toyohashi City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties''' * Lion Heads: Created during the Nanboku-chō to Muromachi periods<ref name=bunkazaicenter />. * Demon Faces: From the Muromachi period<ref name=bunkazaicenter />. * Mikoshi Ridgepole Plaques: Five plaques dating from 1547 (Tenbun 16) to 1852 (Kaei 5)<ref name=yoshidajinja />. * Pair of Lions: From the Kamakura period<ref name=yoshidajinja />.

== Cultural Properties == [[File:吉田天王社祭礼図.jpg|thumb|580x580px|Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting]] '''Toyohashi City Designated Folk Cultural Properties''' * Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting (including donor inscriptions)<ref name=bunkazaicenter>{{Cite web|date=May 7, 2021|url=http://www.toyohashi-bihaku.jp/?p=14012|title=Yoshida Shrine Items Designated as City Cultural Properties|publisher=Toyohashi City Museum of Art and History|accessdate=April 19, 2022}}</ref> : Dimensions: approximately 1.5 m by 3.6 m<ref name=higashiaichi1>{{Cite web|date=May 8, 2021|url=http://www.higashiaichi.co.jp/news/detail/7922|title="Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting" Designated as a Folk Cultural Property|publisher=Higashi Aichi Newspaper|accessdate=April 19, 2022}}</ref>. Painted by Hatake Achi in 1890 (Meiji 23). The painting depicts festival scenes from the late Edo to early Meiji periods.
'''Toyohashi City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties''' * Lion Heads: Created during the Nanboku-chō to Muromachi periods<ref name=bunkazaicenter />. * Demon Faces: From the Muromachi period<ref name=bunkazaicenter />. * Mikoshi Ridgepole Plaques: Five plaques dating from 1547 (Tenbun 16) to 1852 (Kaei 5)<ref name=yoshidajinja />. * Pair of Lions: From the Kamakura period<ref name=yoshidajinja />.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yoshida-jinja (Toyohashi)]][[ja:吉田神社 (豊橋市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q24898358]] Wikidata name: Yoshida-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yoshida Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:吉田神社（豊橋）拝殿.JPG |address = [[2 Sekiya-cho, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture|愛知県豊橋市関屋町2番地]] |coordinates = {{Coord|34.77027777777778|N|137.38991666666666|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] |Rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] |established = (Tradition) [[:en:702 AD|702 AD]] (Taihō 2) |main_hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] |alias = |annual_festival = [[July]] (third [[Friday]], [[Saturday]], and [[Sunday]]) |rituals = Yoritomo Procession |map = {{location map|Japan Aichi Prefecture|label=Yoshida Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} |location = {{ill|Toyohashi|WD=Q336431}} }} '''Yoshida Shrine''' (吉田神社) is a Shinto shrine located at {{ill|Sekiya-cho|ja|関屋町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekiya-cho|1=Sekiya-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109596689}}, Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture. Under the [[Modern typeing System|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was designated a Prefectural Shrine in [[:en:1921|1921]], having been elevated from a {{ill|village shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Deities == The primary deity enshrined is: * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (素戔嗚尊)<ref>{{Cite book|title=Rediscovering Our Hometown Guidebook: Learning More About Toyohashi, Vol. 4|date=March 19, 2009|publisher=Toyohashi City Public Relations Division|page=3}}</ref> Additionally, [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] is enshrined as a complementary deity.
== History == [[File:Yoshida, Tenno Festival on the Fifteenth Day of the Sixth Month LACMA M.66.35.23.jpg|thumb|Yoshida Shrine Tennō Festival]] The shrine is said to originate from the temporary palace established during Empress Jitō's visit to Mikawa Province in 702 (Taihō 2). Following her passing, the palace was converted into a shrine dedicated to her. Early records, such as the ''Wakan Sansai Zue,'' refer to it as a "Tennō Shrine enshrining Empress Jitō." During the Edo period, shrine legends record ceremonies honoring her.
In 1124 (Tenji 1), during an epidemic, the {{ill|Yokochi clan|ja|横地氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokochi clan|qq=}}, shrine priests, invited [[Gozu Tennō]], the guardian deity of [[:en:Jetavana|Jetavana monastery]], from the {{ill|Kanshin-in|ja|感神院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanshin-in|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園信仰|1=Gion Faith|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588425}} (present-day [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]]) to suppress the disease. Subsequently, the shrine was called "Gozu Tennō Shrine" or "Yoshida Tennō Shrine."
In 1178 (Jishō 2), during his exile in Izu, [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] sent an envoy, Suzuki Jūrō Mototoshi, to pray at Yoshida Shrine. In 1186 (Bunji 2), he dispatched Ishida Jirō Tamehisa for the same purpose. During the Kenkyū era, he ordered Adachi Tōkurō Morinaga, the protector of Mikawa, to rebuild the shrine.
In 1507 (Eishō 3), following the construction of [[:en:Yoshida Castle (Mikawa Province)|Yoshida Castle]], {{ill|Makino Kokuhaku|ja|牧野古白|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makino Kokuhaku|1=Makino Kohaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10860041}} rebuilt the shrine as the castle’s guardian shrine. In 1548 (Tenbun 16), [[:en:Imagawa Yoshimoto|Imagawa Yoshimoto]] donated a mikoshi, initiating the shrine’s mikoshi procession. The inscription on the mikoshi’s ridgepole plaque, written by [[:en:Sessai Chōrō|Taigen Sessai]], reads: “Peace throughout the land, safety of the nation, harmony among the people.”
During the Edo period, the shrine received patronage from successive [[:en:Yoshida Domain|lords of Yoshida Domain]], resulting in several reconstructions. In 1837 (Tenpō 6), it received the highest Rank of ''Shoichii'' (Senior First Rank). The shrine’s festivals, influenced by [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]] in Kyoto, were well-known and recorded in various festival Rankings.
Following the abolition of domains during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the shrine’s name changed to Yoshida Shrine under the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}}, and it was reorganized with Susanoo-no-Mikoto as its deity.

== Keidai-sha (境内社) == * Inari-sha (稲荷社) ** Saijin: {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|宇迦之魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} * Izawa-sha (伊雑社) ** Saijin: {{ill|Izawa-no-miya|lt=Isahotomi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊佐波止美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izawa-no-miya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊雑宮}} * Miketsu-sha (御食社) ** Saijin: {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|宇迦之魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, {{ill|Izawa-no-miya|lt=Tamabashiraya-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|玉柱屋比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izawa-no-miya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊雑宮}}
== Cultural Properties == [[File:吉田天王社祭礼図.jpg|thumb|580x580px|Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting]] '''Toyohashi City Designated Folk Cultural Properties''' * Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting (including donor inscriptions)<ref name=bunkazaicenter>{{Cite web|date=May 7, 2021|url=http://www.toyohashi-bihaku.jp/?p=14012|title=Yoshida Shrine Items Designated as City Cultural Properties|publisher=Toyohashi City Museum of Art and History|accessdate=April 19, 2022}}</ref> : Dimensions: approximately 1.5 m by 3.6 m<ref name=higashiaichi1>{{Cite web|date=May 8, 2021|url=http://www.higashiaichi.co.jp/news/detail/7922|title="Yoshida Shrine Traditional Festival Painting" Designated as a Folk Cultural Property|publisher=Higashi Aichi Newspaper|accessdate=April 19, 2022}}</ref>. Painted by Hatake Achi in 1890 (Meiji 23). The painting depicts festival scenes from the late Edo to early Meiji periods.
'''Toyohashi City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties''' * Lion Heads: Created during the Nanboku-chō to Muromachi periods<ref name=bunkazaicenter />. * Demon Faces: From the Muromachi period<ref name=bunkazaicenter />. * Mikoshi Ridgepole Plaques: Five plaques dating from 1547 (Tenbun 16) to 1852 (Kaei 5)<ref name=yoshidajinja />. * Pair of Lions: From the Kamakura period<ref name=yoshidajinja />.
